title: 0 to 100 AI/BI Apps in 120 Days: Building an Enterprise Data and AI Platform From Scratch @ Alpura
abstract: In early 2025, Alpura faced fragmented data, multiple BI tools, weak governance and no scalable AI foundation. In just 120 days, we rebuilt the entire data platform on Databricks. Using the Medallion Architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold), Unity Catalog for enterprise governance and Databricks Metrics for a centralized semantic layer, we created a governed, scalable lakehouse foundation. We consolidated more than five legacy platforms without disrupting operations and eliminated KPI inconsistencies by standardizing business logic. On top of this foundation, we rapidly delivered 100+ AI/BI assets, including 10+ production-grade applications such as a Walmart Scintilla analytics app and an Ambassador Program app — built in days, not months. Leveraging Genie AI, we enabled conversational BI to accelerate adoption. This session shares a practical blueprint for building an AI/BI factory at enterprise speed.

title: 800TB, Zero Pipelines: How Ensemble Health Partners Built an AI-Ready Revenue Cycle on Lakebase
abstract: Ensemble Health Partners manages revenue cycle operations for 30+ health systems, processing 25 billion+ transactions across 800TB of healthcare financial data. Legacy SQL Server sprawl —1 5 individual databases with separate schemas —c reated silos that blocked cross-client insights and slowed AI innovation in an industry where providers operate on 1% margins. This session shows how Ensemble consolidated onto Databricks Lakebase as their AI-ready transactional layer, eliminating brittle pipelines and enabling self-service pods to materialize views directly from harmonized Delta tables. The results: 3–5% net revenue yield improvement ($30M+ per $1B health system), 20% organizational efficiency gain, and 10x reduction in failure rates — while powering agentic AI workflows for denial prevention, AR follow-up, and payer-behavior prediction at scale.

title: Accelerating AI From Model to Impact at Workday With Databricks Apps
abstract: Workday needed to put trusted ML insights directly in the hands of sales, customer success and finance—fast. Before, delivering a single internal app required a bespoke full‑stack build and repeated reviews, stretching timelines to nearly a year. By shifting to Databricks Apps and building where the data already lives, Workday compressed delivery to weeks, launched three production AI applications in a single year, and scaled its flagship Sales Companion to 4,500+ active users. This session details the architecture and adoption playbook: Databricks Apps as the UI/middleware, MLflow‑powered components, retrieval‑augmented workflows for unstructured content, governed access with Unity Catalog and fast analytics via SQL Serverless—enabling conversational experiences that let business users self‑serve sentiment analysis, forecasting and KPI insights without waiting on the data science team or adding headcount.

title: Accelerating Corteva’s Data Source Onboarding With Lakeflow
abstract: Onboarding a new data source at Corteva used to take 30–45 days. Now it’s 4–7 days, about 85% faster. The Lakeflow-first strategy and Unity Catalog helped standardize the ingestion‑to‑consumption pathway across 6 domains. Lakeflow Connect, Spark Declarative Pipelines and Jobs make pipeline runs repeatable and config‑driven. A lightweight metadata layer centralizes data cleansing, and an incremental DDL tool pushes schema changes safely through each environment.This pattern now underpins Corteva’s operational analytics. It accelerates root‑cause analysis linking field variability to dryer‑bin stabilization, improves throughput across 35 seed sites, and strengthens seed quality. Today, the setup covers 5,000+ tables from 15+ sources, shortening time‑to‑insight and speeding partner onboarding. Attendees will leave with a reusable Lakeflow‑first ingestion pattern, a metadata‑driven data quality blueprint and a safe schema‑evolution workflow across environments.

title: Accelerating the Future of Mobility: Unlock Real-Time Data Streaming for Connected Vehicle Platform
abstract: Connected vehicle data is utilized in various services that benefit society, such as disaster response and road condition monitoring. However, processing connected data through high-speed streaming while ensuring privacy and governance is not straightforward, due to the inherent characteristics of connected data and the need to handle PB-scale volumes. Toyota similarly found that its existing architecture, which utilized Snowflake and AWS services, could not meet all its requirements. By adopting Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipeline with Liquid Clustering, they were able to solve this challenge, achieving a 25% performance improvement and 24% cost reduction compared to the existing service. We will share the results of this trial and present our future outlook toward autonomous driving and zero accidents, enabled by these achievements.

title: Agent Evaluations
abstract: This hands-on course introduces learners to evaluating, governing, and securing agentic AI systems on Databricks. You'll begin by exploring the motivation for evaluation and governance frameworks and how they connect to the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. Next, you'll learn how to apply MLflow evaluation metrics and a variety of evaluation techniques, including online evaluation, synthetic evaluation, and building evaluation datasets from MLflow traces. The course then examines how Unity Catalog governance extends to agents' functions, models, and tools, ensuring proper access control, auditability, and compliance. Finally, you'll learn Databricks AI Security Framework (DASF) and how to secure agents with Mosaic AI Gateway, applying guardrails, rate limits, and policy filters to enforce safe and reliable usage. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including Unity Catalog for governance and model management Intermediate Python programming experience Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including RAG architectures and their components (chunking, embeddings, vector stores) Basic knowledge of MLflow for experiment tracking and model lifecycle management General awareness of data governance principles, including access control, data lineage, and data privacy

title: AI Agents for the Frontline: 7-Eleven’s GenAI Maintenance Assistant
abstract: Maintenance technicians at 7-Eleven rely on preventive maintenance, catalogue and parts documents for equipment like HVACs and ovens — often using only their phones. These documents span thousands of files in formats like PDFs and spreadsheets stored in shared drives, making it hard to find the right information quickly. High attrition and missed training sessions compounds knowledge gaps. To address this, 7-Eleven developed a GenAI agentic system using Mosaic AI, Agent Bricks and Databricks Hybrid Search that enables technicians to 1) Retrieve relevant maintenance documents with concise answers 2) Capture equipment images and receive troubleshooting solutions 3) Access parts information by equipment brand 4) Get solutions from the internet. This chatbot bridges knowledge gaps with instant access to institutional knowledge. We will walk through how we built this system, our evaluation methodologies and the three-step prompt framework including guardrails for safe internet searches.

title: Aladdin and Genie — the Ultimate Engineering and Insights Duo
abstract: In Aladdin, the Genie can grant any wish — but only within clear rules. Databricks Genie brings similar magic to data: users ask questions in plain language and instantly receive insights. But as with any powerful tool, the real challenge is not what it can do, but how it should be used. This session explores real-world use cases for Databricks Genie and the governance controls that keep its magic safe, reliable and compliant. Through the lens of Aladdin and Genie, we show how organizations can unlock fast, self-service analytics while protecting data quality, security, and trust — turning raw wishes into responsible decisions.

title: Apps Factory on Databricks​: Provision, Standardize, Observe and Govern 100+ Apps​
abstract: Databricks Apps makes it easy to publish the first app. We address the compounding challenges organizations face when scaling to 10, 50, or 100+ Databricks Apps: inconsistent architecture, unclear access control, missing standards, ungoverned AI components and runaway costs. We will walk through a comprehensive methodology, including solutions and principles to provision, govern and observe dozens of applications at enterprise scale. The session includes reference architecture, frameworks, templates, a production readiness checklist, CI/CD setup, security & access control, unified logging and cost tracking. Attendees receive a comprehensive technical governance and adaptation playbook to effectively manage and scale app ecosystems.

title: AstraZeneca's Multi-Agent System: Lessons Scaling Agents by 10x With Agent Bricks
abstract: AstraZeneca built a multi-agent system using Agent Bricks to transform how commercial teams access pharmaceutical data. The Brand Assistant uses a supervisor agent coordinating specialized sub-agents across therapeutic areas, combining structured data via Genie Spaces with unstructured documents via Knowledge Assistant. Unity Catalog integration with Entra ID enforces strict permission boundaries. MCP unlocks third-party tool integration.The architecture scaled from 5-agent POC to 20+ agents in production, designed for 50+. Key components: Multi-Agent Supervisor for orchestration Genie Spaces for NL-to-SQL Knowledge Assistant for documents Unity Catalog for row/column-level security MLflow for tracing. This session covers: Multi-agent architecture patterns Permission-preserving design When to split supervisors vs. add agents Human-in-the-loop testing Why bad data breaks agents regardless of tech Organizational challenges of agent ownership Lessons from scaling

title: Atlassian's Journey of Using AI/BI Genie From Pilot to Production and What's Next?
abstract: This session is not a generic “AI is the future” talk. It is a practical, experience‑based guide to taking Databricks AI/BI Genie from a promising pilot to a trusted, production‑grade capability. Dashboards answer “what,” but not “why” or “what if.” Here is where Genie comes in.We’ll walk through our journey at Atlassian from pilot to production—designing Genie spaces on curated tables and metrics, building an evaluation framework for accuracy and coverage, and integrating Genie into our everyday tools and Rovo AI agent. We will touch upon how Databricks and Atlassian partnered to bring the power of Genie to our Rovo Marketplace and built the ability to route a user question to the right Genie space.In the end attendees will have a concrete “0‑to‑95*” playbook for unleashing their data with Genie to reach a point where roughly 95% of everyday questions can be answered accurately, safely, and self‑service freeing data teams to focus on higher‑value work and empowering faster decisions.

title: Automate Deployment with Databricks Asset Bundles
abstract: This hands-on course covers DevOps principles, including CI/CD and testing, applied to Databricks data engineering pipelines. It focuses on continuous deployment using the Databricks REST API, SDK, and CLI, specifically incorporating Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs) for streamlined deployment across environments. You will learn to modify, validate, deploy, and execute DABs for multiple configurations using the Databricks CLI. The course also introduces using Visual Studio Code as an IDE for local development and concludes with automating deployment using GitHub Actions and DABs. By the end, you'll be able to automate Databricks project deployments with DABs, improving efficiency through DevOps practices. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Strong familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform Intermediate Python programming experience, including designing and implementing functions and classes Intermediate SQL experience for data analysis and transformation Experience with data ingestion and transformation using PySpark Familiarity with DevOps practices and basic Git version control

title: Automating KPI Reasoning and Narrative Generation With Agent Bricks and Databricks Apps
abstract: Monthly performance reporting previously required approximately 7 person-days each month, as analysts manually wrote queries, reviewed distributed datasets, interpreted metric relationships and produced presentation-ready reports. This repetitive process tied up analytical resources and slowed decision-making. To address this, I built an app-based automation pipeline using multi-supervised agents with Agent Bricks and Databricks Apps. The agent operates as an analyst, systematically analyzing relevant data sources through a structured, table-driven representation to identify key drivers, evaluate metric relationships, and generate narrative insights without manual querying. Outputs are delivered through Databricks Apps as a self-serve analytics platform with automatically refreshed metrics and visualizations, eliminating repetitive reporting work, reducing monthly effort by approximately 7 person-days, and enabling agent-authored reporting for both analytics and non-analytics stakeholders.

title: Beacon: Revolutionizing Media Analytics With Near Real-Time Declarative Pipelines
abstract: CondéNast operates in a digital first media landscape where editorial and business decisions are defined in minutes. To meet this need, we built Beacon, a near real time media analytics platform that transformed a fragmented, high latency analytics stack into a unified architecture powered by the Databricks Lakehouse and Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines.Having InfluxDB and Qlik solution, introduced a 15 minutes data lag and inconsistent KPIs. It was re-architected by streaming data directly from web collectors into Databricks, unifying streaming and batch processing through declarative pipelines. A Redis-based caching layer enables sub second access while preserving the Lakehouse as the system of record.This approach reduced end-to-end latency to under 10 seconds, enabling editorial, revenue and growth teams to monitor article performance, live events and monetisation signals in near real time through a single analytics experience. This will be enabled with Genie LLM in future roadmap.

title: Beyond Batch: Engineering Self-Evolving Ingestion with Databricks Auto Loader
abstract: Is your data engineering team trapped in brittle schema fixes and rising costs? As data sources multiply, traditional ingestion stalls enterprise insights. At Capital One Software, we’ve moved beyond static ETL to a “self-evolving” framework that treats data as a dynamic stream. This session reveals our modular architecture using Databricks Auto Loader to bridge multi-platform data into S3 with zero manual overhead. We’ll dive into how Schema Evolution and Rescue Columns handle upstream drift automatically, keeping pipelines live when source systems change. Key takeaways: Modular design: Decouple ingestion from transformation for reusable, enterprise-scale patterns Dynamic schema management: Strategies to detect and adapt to drift without breaking dependencies Cost optimization: Real-world tactics for balancing trigger intervals and compute for peak efficiency. Transition from reactive maintenance to automated data empowerment

title: Beyond Medallion: Architecting Disney’s DATOS for Complex, Real-Time Data Streams With Databricks
abstract: Disney’s Medallion architecture laid a strong foundation for enterprise data governance, but emerging needs—ranging from personalized user experiences to real-time financial insights—required a richer, near real-time view across domains such as subscriptions, billing and payments. DATOS addresses this by serving as a contextual aggregation and abstraction layer built on the Databricks Lakehouse Platform. Using Structured Streaming and Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines, DATOS ingests diverse sources like Kafka, Kinesis, and data lakes while absorbing upstream schema and delivery variability to provide stable, reusable data products. Liquid Clustering enhances performance on high‑volume tables, and Unity Catalog ensures unified governance and security. This session explores how DATOS reduces operational overhead, decouples producers and consumers, accelerates onboarding, and delivers a context-aware source of truth that powers real-time analytics, Unity Dashboards, and advanced AI/ML workloads.
description_raw: Disney’s Medallion architecture laid a strong foundation for enterprise data governance, but emerging needs—ranging from personalized user experiences to real-time financial insights—required a richer, near real-time view across domains such as subscriptions, billing and payments. DATOS addresses this by serving as a contextual aggregation and abstraction layer built on the Databricks Lakehouse Platform. Using Structured Streaming and Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines, DATOS ingests diverse sources like Kafka, Kinesis, and data lakes while absorbing upstream schema and delivery variability to provide stable, reusable data products. Liquid Clustering enhances performance on high‑volume tables, and Unity Catalog ensures unified governance and security. This session explores how DATOS reduces operational overhead, decouples producers and consumers, accelerates onboarding, and delivers a context-aware source of truth that powers real-time analytics, Unity Dashboards, and advanced AI/ML workloads.

title: Beyond the Trace: adidas’ Agent Digital Twin for Governance, Cost and ROI
abstract: Your agent answered but can you prove why, what it cost, and what breaks when a tool or prompt changes? AI spend is forecast near $1.5T in 2025 and GenAI at $644B, yet Gartner predicts >40% of agentic AI projects will be canceled by end-2027 as costs rise and controls lag. At adidas (200+ serving endpoints, 300+ data and AI products, 6k+ registered models, 600k+ pipeline runs), we go beyond per-run traces to an agent digital twin: A lakehouse control plane mapping agent → tool → prompt → retrieval → model call → post-processing, then rolling up risk, quality and unit economics across a fleet. You’ll see a Databricks pattern with MLflow Tracing, Unity Catalog + system tables for audit evidence and DBU/cost signals, plus eval/optimization loops with governance guardrails. Key highlights: Digital-twin control plane for fleets Per-hop unit economics + cost leak forensics Continuous audit evidence by design ROI attribution by agent behavior

title: Bionic Supply Chain: Architecting a Unified Agentic Framework for Risk & Cost Recovery
abstract: The modern supply chain is fractured, stuck in a “visibility vacuum” of cost asymmetry and a “triage trap” where material and production planners miss critical risks and purchasing managers miss cost targets due to cognitive overload. To bridge this gap, we present a unified vision for the AI-enabled supply chain ecosystem, moving beyond disparate tools to a cohesive intelligent platform. We introduce an agentic architecture built entirely on the lakehouse, utilizing Databricks Apps and Unity Catalog to serve distinct personas. For purchasing managers, we deploy NOVAA, an engine using MLflow to benchmark “should-costs” and Mosaic AI to arrest margin leakage and generate negotiation playbooks. Simultaneously, SONAR, our supply planners’ control tower, empowers them by aggregating SAP/TMS data into a unified truth. Here, a hierarchical agentic workflow, powered by Agent Bricks, bridges classical ML with GenAI to predict disruptions and orchestrate sub-agents for autonomous resolution.

title: BP Accelerates Maintenance Decisions With Conversational Analytics With Genie
abstract: BP, a global energy company, operates complex, asset-intensive environments where maintenance, reliability, safety and logistics decisions must be made quickly and with confidence. Across refineries and sites, teams rely on data from dozens of systems, including SAP and multiple operational tools, to plan maintenance work, manage materials and ensure equipment safety. Before Databricks AI/BI Genie, accessing this information required deep system knowledge, manual coordination across teams, long request queues and weeks of engineering effort to produce custom reports. With AI/BI Genie, accessed via Databricks One, BP introduced a simple, natural language interface to its maintenance data, giving a large number of business users an intuitive way to access insights without bottlenecking on a small number of colleagues with deep technical expertise.

title: BP Cut Cloud Spend by 43% and Speed Critical Jobs From Days to ~8 Hours With Databricks SQL
abstract: BP is a 117-year-old global energy company spanning upstream exploration and production, refining, trading, retail, and growing low-carbon businesses. Its data estate evolved across classic enterprise warehouse patterns and cloud stacks split by business unit—upstream on Azure and retail on AWS. Hadoop-era systems became too slow at scale, and Redshift-based serving was costly and hard to right-size. BP standardized on Databricks SQL as its warehouse and BI serving layer across clouds, using Unity Catalog and Delta Sharing to reuse governed data without copying it. The team reduced cloud cost 43% and accelerated key processing from days to ~8 hours, while powering Power BI and other tools through Databricks SQL Serverless warehouses.

title: Breaking the Silos: Data Fusion for Financial Crime and Cyber Security
abstract: Traditional silos between cybersecurity, payments and fraud detection often hide the "connective tissue" of modern threats. By adopting a Databricks-based SIEM 2.0 and an Instrumentation Data Lake, NAB has enabled "Data Fusion"—the ability to instantly correlate a spike in payment latency with specific security threat vectors. This talk outlines the "hub-and-spoke" governance model used to map common entities across 50+ distinct sources, enabling a unified security posture that enhances the firm’s safety without stifling innovation.

title: Bringing Agents to Data: How Workday Architects Governed Agentic AI with Iceberg and Unity Catalog
abstract: Traditional “data-to-compute” models create latency and fragmented governance that hinder Agentic AI. To solve this, Workday and Databricks advocate “bringing agents to data” via the Databricks Lakehouse. By operating on data in situ, autonomous agents maintain real-time consistency without the risks of replication. This architecture enables reliable multi-agent coordination at scale while ensuring unified data integrity and reduced operational costs across the enterprise. The technical core uses Apache Iceberg for ACID transactions and snapshot isolation, providing a stable foundation for agent reasoning. Databricks Unity Catalog adds a unified governance layer, enforcing fine-grained security, lineage, and auditability. This production-grade approach significantly cuts query latency and storage expenses, allowing organizations to scale from single-agent pilots to hundreds of coordinated agents across multiple departments like Customer Experience, RevOps and Analytics.

title: Build Data Pipelines with Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines
abstract: This hands-on course introduces users to the essential concepts and skills needed to build data pipelines using Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) in Databricks for incremental batch or streaming ingestion and processing through multiple streaming tables and materialized views. Designed for data engineers new to Spark Declarative Pipelines, the course provides a comprehensive overview of core components such as incremental data processing, streaming tables, materialized views, and temporary views, highlighting their specific purposes and differences. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces, Apache Spark, Delta Lake, Medallion Architecture, Lakeflow Jobs, and Unity Catalog Experience ingesting raw data into Delta tables using the read_files SQL function (CSV, JSON, TXT, Parquet) Intermediate SQL proficiency, including intermediate-level queries and joins Understanding of ETL concepts and batch/streaming workflows

title: Building “Vehicle 360” at Daimler Truck & Bus — How We Find Needles in a 30,000 Parameter Haystack
abstract: Daimler Truck & Bus built “Vehicle 360” to break down organizational silos on a global scale and create one trusted, up‑to‑date view of each vehicle. We combine configuration and production records, sales and aftersales processes, over‑the‑air updates and more than 300 GB of daily vehicle log files to maintain this view across more than 1,000,000 vehicles — each with 30,000+ parameters. On an open lakehouse architecture, Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines keep data fresh and organized, while Unity Catalog provides unified governance and easy sharing.It gives teams one place to see a vehicle’s current state, drill into recent parameter changes, find vehicles with specific characteristics, orchestrate dynamic over‑the‑air updates, and diagnose issues faster. Beyond day‑one wins, this foundation also enables proactive anomaly detection, AI‑driven support and our journey to a software‑defined vehicle.

title: Building a Data Telescope to Study the Vast Expanse of Social Media
abstract: Researchers studying extremism and misinformation need massive social media datasets but face infrastructure and budget constraints. Traditional solutions offer dashboard access to pre-aggregated data, limiting flexibility. Princeton’s Accelerator gives researchers direct Databricks workspace access with pre-built Typical Activity Datasets, enabling custom queries and analysis. We built a multi-tenant data and AI-powered platform serving R1 institutions.Our medallion architecture ingests data via Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines: Bronze captures raw data, Silver applies filtering/text enrichments, and researchers can query via semantic search powered by MLOps. Leveraging Databricks Asset Bundles, REST APIs, and Unity Catalog, we built solutions for cross-workspace monitoring. Users who spent months building scrapers start work in days, querying 100+ languages and discovering content patterns while learning valuable skills. This talk shares our architecture for democratizing data infrastructure with engineering rigor.

title: Building a Financial Services Customer 360 and Agentic Decision Engine on Databricks
abstract: Barclays, a global financial services giant, is overhauling its customer data strategy to unify data across multiple business units and enable next-generation personalization. Join us as the Barclays Enterprise Data Platform (EDP) team and Databricks discuss their journey to build an “Open Party Platform"” (C360) on the Databricks Lakehouse. This talk will cover how they are tackling critical, high-pain challenges including: mitigating vendor lock-in from legacy systems, achieving true cross-business unit data standardization and implementing advanced identity resolution and linking using machine learning (including GNNs). Furthermore, they will delve into the architecture for a future-proof, agent-based decisioning engine that powers unified customer outreach and cross-sell/upsell opportunities, all while ensuring low-latency data serving for operational use cases.

abstract: Michael Uohara—CDAO at HSS has gone all in with Databricks taking HSS's 30 data sources and ingesting them into Databricks. title: Building a Production: Scale Dimensional Data Mart With Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines and AUTO CDC
abstract: At 84.51˚, we replaced legacy ETL processing in our retail sales data mart with Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines to simplify development while improving reliability at scale. In this session, we’ll walk through how a low-code, declarative approach enables dimensional modeling for both slowly changing dimensions and high-volume fact data.You’ll see how to use AUTO CDC to handle data updates across batch and streaming pipelines, supporting continuously arriving sales transactions alongside rapidly changing product, store and customer attributes. Our ETL crunches approximately 8 million sales transactions and over 80 million purchased items per day in support of our 84.51˚ Stratum platform.Attendees will leave with practical guidance for designing and operating dimensional data marts using Spark Declarative Pipelines, including patterns for CDC, schema evolution, and hybrid batch/streaming workloads that can be applied immediately in production environments.

title: Building a Unified Data Platform: easyJet's Strategic Evolution With Databricks
abstract: Three years ago, easyJet's data estate was primarily focused on centralizing data from multiple operational systems, with most workloads running in batch and limited real-time capability.This session describes the strategic evolution of easyJet's Data Strategy by adopting Databricks as a single enterprise foundation for data and AI across the organization. It will cover the transition from basic data consolidation to supporting streaming and near-real-time processing, enabling teams to work from a shared, governed Enterprise data platform.The session will also explain how Lakebase was introduced to support low-latency access patterns alongside analytical workloads.Finally, the talk outlines how Databricks was integrated with surrounding cloud and enterprise tooling to form a reference architecture that supports scalability, governance and reuse across the organization, and how this has strengthened easyJet's data estate as a single strategic technology platform.

title: Building Agentic Applications on Databricks
abstract: This hands-on course introduces how to build single-agent, multi-agent, and multi-model systems on Agent Bricks, including key components, architectures, and use cases. You'll learn how to create agent tools using Unity Catalog functions to extend agent capabilities. You'll begin by using Genie to build agents that can interact with structured data, before extending these concepts to create a second agent designed for working with unstructured data and external resources. Finally, you'll explore how Agent Bricks provides a declarative approach to building multi-agent applications, offering a streamlined alternative to manual development. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces and notebooks Intermediate Python and SQL proficiency Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including LLMs and prompting strategies Familiarity with Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture Familiarity with API usage and function calling for LLM interactions with external tools and services

title: Building Agentic Business Applications on Databricks with Lakebase, MCP and Marvis AI
abstract: Many organizations use Databricks for analytics and AI, but still build business applications on separate stacks, disconnected from the lakehouse. We share how we use Databricks as a full application platform, combining analytics, operational data and AI into production-grade systems. We will show how we built DNA Port and Marvis AI using Databricks Apps, Lakebase, Lakeflow Sync Tables and MLflow for governed model lifecycle management. We will also demonstrate how MCP securely exposes trusted lakehouse data to AI copilots. abstract: Rippling leverages Databricks to power agentic GTM operations across RevOps, PLG, marketing, sales and account management. title: Building an Explainable AI Research Agent With Databricks Genie
abstract: Forecasting sales at more than 7,000 retail stores generates frequent inquiries into forecast drivers, and traditional methods like driver dashboards or SHAP fail to give field leaders clear narratives. Data scientists were spending days writing ad‑hoc SQL to answer basic “why” questions. In partnership with Databricks, we built an AI/BI Genie-powered Research Agent that delivers fast, plain‑language forecast explanations using governed data. The agent joins core datasets, computes variances and accuracy metrics, correlates key drivers and links every answer to underlying SQL, cutting analysis time from days to minutes and revealing model biases. This session covers how we used Unity Catalog as the semantic backbone and encoded business rules in the Genie Knowledge Store so the agent reasons like a retail analyst. We’ll demo the agent with Deep Research and share key lessons and a repeatable blueprint for operationalizing Genie.

title: Building Enterprise Applications with Databricks Apps
abstract: This hands-on course introduces Databricks Apps, a new feature that empowers developers to build and deploy secure data and AI applications directly on the Databricks platform. It is primarily designed for data scientists, data engineers, and developers who need to create custom applications that make data insights accessible to non-technical users within their organizations. The course begins with an overview of the main components of Databricks Apps, followed by the deployment of a simple app using available templates. Next, you will learn how to build and deploy a data-driven application that integrates with other platform features. The course then covers how to set up an IDE for Databricks Apps, and how to edit and deploy an app using an IDE. It concludes with how to implement security and best practices for an enterprise app. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. title: Building Enterprise-Scale Agentic Claims Automation and AI Observability With Databricks
abstract: As enterprises move from GenAI experimentation to production, the hardest problems are no longer models or agent frameworks—they are enterprise‑scale orchestration and observability.At Suncorp, we have deployed 16 production‑grade agentic AI solutions that are now converging into an enterprise claims automation capability. This session shares the reference architectures and engineering patterns behind this journey, focusing on two pillars: agentic orchestration, where Databricks supports low‑latency, governed workflows and shared agent memory using Delta Lake, Vector Search, Agent Bricks, and Lakebase, and AI observability at scale, where we use MLflow evaluation, OpenTelemetry, Databricks Apps, and AI/BI Dashboards to trace agent behaviour, decisions, quality and risk.Attendees will leave with practical patterns for building orchestrated, observable agentic AI in a regulated enterprise.

title: Building Query Expert MCP: Using RAG to Build an Analytics Agent That Goes Beyond Text-to-SQL
abstract: LLMs are fast and confident SQL writers that can unlock self-serve analytics across your business. But actionable insights require more than SQL syntax—they demand business context, domain expertise and nuances that live outside your schema docs.In this talk, we'll deep-dive into how Block built Query Expert MCP, a scalable AI agent that captures and distributes institutional data knowledge using Databricks. You'll learn how we: Automated documentation at scale—using query_ai sub-agents to generate standardized table documentation across 11,000+ tables Captured SQL nuances—Databricks sub-agents categorize and label domain-specific query patterns across 1M sample queries Human in the loop—Databricks Apps allow users to directly edit, remove or upload context to the agent’s memory Enabled intelligent retrieval—Databricks Vector Search indexes for RAG-powered query generation that scales to a 12,000 person org, while supporting domain-specific scoping for teams' use cases

title: Building RAG Agents with Agent Bricks
abstract: This hands-on course teaches you how to build retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) agents on Databricks using Agent Bricks’ tooling—including Vector Search, AI Playground, and the Foundation Model APIs. First, you'll begin with prompt engineering and the fundamentals of retrieval and augmented generation. Next, you'll learn how to ingest, process, and prepare documents for use in a retrieval pipeline. Then you'll explore embedding vectors, vector database design, and how to use Vector Search in a Databricks environment. Finally, you will build a retrieval agent end-to-end and trace its behavior using MLflow Tracing for observability and debugging. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including Unity Catalog Intermediate Python programming experience Basic SQL proficiency for querying and creating functions Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including large language models (LLMs) and prompt engineering Basic knowledge of MLflow

title: Building the Commercial Lakehouse: AI-Driven, Open and Interoperable
abstract: AstraZeneca modernized its Commercial data estate, transitioning from legacy EMR to a high-performance global commercial lakehouse. At the core lies Unity Catalog (UC), established as the unified governance layer. We demonstrate how UC enables a true open architecture, orchestrating seamless federation for legacy Glue workloads and utilizing Iceberg REST integration to provide zero-copy access for iceberg clients e.g., Snowflake.The transformation was powered by a multi-agent AI migration system, utilizing LLMs and testing agents to automate the conversion of 250+ pipelines into a rigorous Medallion architecture. Key components include Databricks Asset Bundles for versioned CI/CD, Liquid Clustering for storage optimization.Processing complex external data sources (Claims, CRM, Digital), this platform delivered 50% cost savings. This session covers the multi-agent migration architecture, UC-centric interoperability design patterns and lessons from modernizing commercial data at scale.

title: Built on Databricks: How CMSPI Scaled Payments Routing and Optimization Using Lakebase and DBSQL
abstract: CMSPI built a Databricks-native analytics app that automates payments optimization at enterprise scale — replacing spreadsheets with a governed platform that processes billions of transactions and delivers measurable time and cost savings for global merchants. Lakebase powers real-time scenario configuration, while Delta tables and SQL warehouses enable interactive analytics. Databricks Workflows orchestrate a mathematical solver (PROM) that evaluates billions of routing combinations and returns results in 30–40 minutes — eliminating 30+ hours of manual work per initial analysis and uncovering ~10% incremental savings opportunities. This session shares CMSPI’s path to productization: access control with Entra ID groups, MLOps for approval-rate prediction models and external monetization via Delta Sharing to non-Databricks consumers. The architecture demonstrates the “built on Databricks” pattern for secure, governed apps — with plans to expand collaboration using Databricks Clean Rooms.

title: Creating Better Travel Experiences for All — Confronting Airline Pricing Pressures From Legacy Tools
abstract: easyJet carried nearly 90 million passengers last year, flying more than 1,200 routes, with a fleet of 300+ planes. Even as one of Europe’s leading low-cost airlines, the company faced mounting pressure to modernize, as traveler expectations necessitate seamless booking, flexible changes and personalized offers. Across Europe, high costs, thin margins and digital-first competitors only raised the stakes for all airlines. With core systems still tied to a decade-old desktop app and one of the region’s largest SQL Server databases, easyJet struggled with frequent engineering bottlenecks, fragmented workflows and more. Moving to the Databricks Platform helped the airline consolidate their commercial tools into a single hub and cut deployment timelines from up to nine months to three — a pace shift that put innovation back on track for easyJet.

title: Data Governance at Scale with ABAC at Atlassian
abstract: Atlassian’s internal data platform is the backbone for analytics, product insights and business decisions across the company. Managing data access policies at this scale is complex. In this talk, we’ll share how Atlassian embeds robust data governance policies, balancing agility with compliance, and innovation with security. Learn how we handle access management, data classification, and policy enforcement across diverse teams and tools. A key part of our approach is leveraging Databricks Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) to manage and abstract complex business policies. ABAC enables us to flexibly enforce fine-grained access controls (FGAC) using data tagging along with tag-driven policies, adapting quickly to changing requirements while keeping implementation logic abstracted from users. Join us for insights into the challenges, lessons learned, and solutions that keep our data platform secure and well-governed.

Learn how we handle access management, data classification, and policy enforcement across diverse teams and tools. ABAC enables us to flexibly enforce fine-grained access controls (FGAC) using data tagging along with tag-driven policies, adapting quickly to changing requirements while keeping implementation logic abstracted from users. title: Databricks Apps: A Magic Wand for Driving Data Quality Adoption and Observability
abstract: Most data quality tools don’t fail because the checks are wrong. They fail because users don’t understand how to use them.In this talk, we’ll share how we rebuilt our internal data quality platform using Databricks Apps and Databricks Workflows table triggers to prioritize usability and holistic visibility. We moved away from a config-file-driven model owned by a small group of platform engineers toward a user-centric approach that empowers teams to create, test and manage their own data quality rules. With a Databricks App as the central interface, users can define and test rules, inspect production run history, and track data quality trends across the tables and teams they own. Combined with table-triggered workflows, checks run automatically as data changes without added operational complexity.This session covers our architecture, key design decisions, and lessons learned, and shows how Databricks Apps can turn data infrastructure into a user-friendly platform.

title: Databricks FinOps Genie — Cost Observability Meets Optimization Insights
abstract: We will showcase Databricks FinOps Genie, a cost observability and optimization capability that combines a Databricks‑native FinOps dashboard, Genie space and FinOps agent, proven at scale across 1,000+ workspaces in a large bank. Learn how to turn Databricks usage and billing data into a lightweight, reusable cost intelligence layer that surfaces key drivers of spend, anomalies and optimization opportunities through a single dashboard. title: Deep Dive Into Streaming and Batch ETLs With Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines
abstract: Let's deep dive into the new declarative ETL pipeline framework in Apache Spark™, Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP). Let's peel it off to learn how SDP's high-level Python and SQL abstractions translate into lower-level Spark SQL and Spark Structured Streaming queries. During the talk, you will learn how SDP automatically resolves complex dependencies and builds optimized Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) for both batch and streaming workloads. We will walk through the internal state management and orchestration logic that allows SDP to handle retries and incremental processing out of the box, replacing thousands of lines of imperative "glue code." You will leave with a clear mental model of the engine's architecture, the pros and cons of SDP, and be ready to debug and optimize your pipelines. You'll understand where and how to use it (alongside your existing ETL pipelines).

title: Delta Sharing: The Most Widely Adopted Open Protocol for Data and AI Sharing
abstract: AI is only as good as the data that fuels it. To build and scale models, organizations need a way to move past fragmented data silos and complex ETL. Delta Sharing provides the industry’s most widely adopted open protocol for the secure, real-time exchange of the datasets and AI assets required for modern innovation. We’ll dive deep into: Fueling AI With Openness: How to share live data, volumes and AI models with any recipient—regardless of their platform—to accelerate model training and deployment. Latest Innovations: A first look at enhanced governance, automated provider workflows and expanded support for complex assets like AI functions and unstructured data. Real-World Scale: Real-world use cases showing how organizations use the protocol to unify their data ecosystems and manage AI assets at scale. From live demos to architectural best practices, you’ll walk away with a roadmap to unify your data ecosystem and lead the next wave of open collaboration.

title: Deploying and Monitoring Agents on Databricks
abstract: This hands-on course guides you through deploying and monitoring agentic AI systems in the Databricks environment. You will start by learning how to deploy agents in batch mode using AI Functions and integrate them into data pipelines. Next, you'll explore real-time deployment using Model Serving, including deploying agents as REST endpoints and managing autoscaling, versioning, and governance via Mosaic AI Model Serving. You'll then turn to observability and monitoring, using MLflow tracing and scoring functions to capture metrics, analyze requests and responses, and detect anomalies in production. Finally, you'll learn best practices for deploying agents with built-in trace collection, inference logging, and feedback loops to support ongoing evaluation and robustness. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including Unity Catalog and Delta Lake Intermediate Python programming experience Familiarity with Generative AI fundamentals, including LLMs, RAG architectures, and prompt engineering Familiarity with Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs) Basic SQL proficiency, including use of functions like ai_query Basic understanding of MLOps/LLMOps principles and software deployment concepts, including CI/CD pipelines, REST APIs, and YAML configuration

title: Developing BI Dashboards with AI/BI
abstract: In this hands-on course, you’ll learn how to use the features Databricks provides for business intelligence needs: AI/BI Dashboards and AI/BI Genie. As a Databricks Data Analyst, you will be tasked with creating AI/BI Dashboards and AI/BI Genie Spaces within the platform, managing the access to these assets by stakeholders and necessary parties, and maintaining these assets as they are edited, refreshed, or decommissioned over the course of their lifespan. title: Doubling Medical Safety: Fine-Tuning Open LLMs for Women's Health Without Human Labels
abstract: Enterprises building LLM features in healthcare hit the same wall: satisfying dozens of safety rules simultaneously—crisis escalation, treatment boundaries, referral language—while real user data is off-limits and expert labeling is prohibitively expensive.We'll show how Flo Health broke through using RFT-inspired synthetic fine-tuning, transforming Llama 3.3 70B into a healthcare-compliant assistant for women's health that doubled safety compliance versus our previous iteration. The key insight: instead of investing expert time in labeling, we redirected it into designing LLM judges that scale.Our system uses 60 LLM judges—52 for medical safety, 8 for usefulness—with priority-weighted reward aggregation where P1 safety rules dominate over P2 quality rules. You'll learn patterns for multi-judge evaluation systems, reward aggregation strategies for binary constraints, and why simpler approaches beat complex alternatives. For anyone building AI where "mostly safe" isn't good enough.

title: Driving Enterprise Transformation With an AI‑Enabled Field Assistant
abstract: Lilly’s presentation will highlight how a dual business and technology strategy is guiding the rollout of an AI field assistant and enabling scalable governance and organizational change management across diverse groups of field users. We will highlight why we chose the platform and how the Databricks ecosystem is accelerating innovation by unifying data, analytics and AI capabilities to support smarter, faster decision making. We expect to share expanded updates on emerging features such as Genie, which will ease prompt constraints and support more abstract, data aware questions, along with new agentic actions that streamline workflows and broaden what users can accomplish through natural language interaction. Throughout the presentation we will share lessons learned from deploying the platform at scale. Together, these advancements illustrate how coordinated business and technical innovation are shaping more adaptive, AI enabled solutions.

title: Embracing Serverless SQL at Riot Games
abstract: At Riot Games, we found that embracing serverless compute—rather than limiting it—allowed us to scale more effectively while improving performance, governance and user experience.In this session, we’ll share Riot’s shift from a cost-first mindset to an enablement-driven approach using Databricks SQL Serverless, with Unity Catalog as a key enabler. Consistent governance, lineage and usage visibility gave us the confidence to adopt serverless while maintaining transparency and accountability.We’ll explore how serverless SQL warehouses support diverse analytics and reporting needs through strong performance, simplified user experiences and elastic, on-demand cost efficiency. We’ll also discuss how serverless compute streamlined the data engineering lifecycle by reducing infrastructure management.Finally, we’ll cover Riot’s broader adoption of serverless for jobs and general workloads, the resulting operational and cost benefits, and how we manage costs in a serverless-first model.

title: Emotional Congruence at Scale: Using Databricks AI to Align Ads and Content
abstract: TV advertisers know context matters, but most contextual signals stop at coarse attributes like genre or keywords. What about emotion? What we watch shapes how we feel—and how we feel influences how we engage with brands.At NBCUniversal, we built an Emotional Congruence engine to quantify whether alignment between these emotional signals drives intent and conversion—and how to scale it using Databricks.Using foundation models served on Databricks, we extract and summarize unstructured signals from content and ads, tagging each with a standardized emotional taxonomy across themes, tones and personal values. Governed end-to-end with MLflow 3.0, the system is orchestrated for rigorous testing and deployment.Testing shows emotional alignment drives measurable lift—often in the double digits. As a result, it is now embedded within NBCUniversal’s broader contextual advertising offering.

title: Empowering Non-Technical Users in Pharma With Dashboards and Chatbots Working Together in Databricks
abstract: The presentation shows how Databricks enables non‑technical users in pharma to gain scientific insights by uniting dashboards, chatbots, documents and data. The foundation is data and documents from source systems organized in Unity Catalog and a semantic layer of schemas, tables with definitions, metrics views with business logics, keys linking datasets, SQL functions, Genie and Agent Bricks. Clinical study data is complex, with interpretation depending on context often described in documents. We use a dashboard for context, with visuals and filters conveying the overarching story, while Genie and Agent Bricks offer insights via natural-language questions. Traditional dashboards excel at data storytelling but lack flexibility and tends to grow into overwhelming complexity, by adding context-aware chatbots we address these challenges. Insights can also be embedded into platforms like Teams and SharePoint, ensuring easy access, while having data and calculations centrally governed.

title: Enabling a Low-Latency Data Mesh at Warner Music Group Using Lakebase, Iceberg and Unity Catalog
abstract: Today’s enterprises face daunting challenges when creating impact with data, from fundamental trust issues to dynamically evolving AI use cases. Legacy systems with technical debt impede business value even as users expect trustworthy, low-latency insights for both natural language queries and classic insights. Furthermore, pipelines are often distributed across different engines. This environment demands a flexible approach to drive impact that brings cohesion to an often-messy data environment. This session describes how WMG Tech leveraged Databricks to develop a declarative data platform that realizes a compositional data mesh designed to tackle this challenge. description_raw:
Today’s enterprises face daunting challenges when creating impact with data, from fundamental trust issues to dynamically evolving AI use cases. Legacy systems with technical debt impede business value even as users expect trustworthy, low-latency insights for both natural language queries and classic insights. Furthermore, pipelines are often distributed across different engines. This environment demands a flexible approach to drive impact that brings cohesion to an often-messy data environment.

This session describes how WMG Tech leveraged Databricks to develop a declarative data platform that realizes a compositional data mesh designed to tackle this challenge. This architecture includes:

  • Iceberg as an engine agnostic data layer for access to diverse sources
  • Unity Catalog for compositional access to data products
  • Lakebase for native low latency analytical APIs

Together, this platform enables an array of data products that serve AI queries and WMG's applications.

title: Essential Ingredients to Successful Digital Data Transformation Program
abstract: GM Financial led a multi‑year data and AI transformation on Databricks, moving from traditional reporting to a modern analytics and GenAI‑enabled ecosystem guided by a clear enterprise vision. This session shares the people and process “recipe” behind that change: formal data governance and operating bodies, a data strategy board, stewardship practices, and communities of practice that increased literacy, trust, and SOX‑ready data. We will highlight the Databricks Center of Excellence—onboarding, study groups, certifications, office hours and best‑practice sharing—that accelerated adoption and improved CSAT/NPS. The talk also introduces AI/BI Genie—a Databricks chatbot that turns natural language into SQL over curated documentation, enabling governed self‑service analytics. Finally, we show how a Databricks‑centric FinOps framework gives pipeline‑to‑query cost visibility, enables show‑back of spend and drives continuous cost optimization without sacrificing agility.

title: Feature and ML Platform Powered by Databricks
abstract: This presentation offers a comprehensive exploration of the Feature & Machine Learning Platform, focusing on its architectural design, essential components and seamless integration with Databricks. The platform harnesses Databricks for advanced feature engineering, structured streaming, declarative pipeline orchestration and end-to-end model training and serving, supporting both scheduled and event-driven workflows for maximum flexibility. Key technologies highlighted include the DBX Feature Engineering SDK, Apache Spark Structured Streaming and Databricks Lakeflow jobs, all of which contribute to scalable, efficient data processing. Online feature computation is facilitated through the DBX Unity Catalog with Python UDFs, while Databricks Online Feature Stores powered by Lakebase enable real-time use cases. For batch feature consumption outside Databricks, managed Delta UniForm and Iceberg read-only feature tables provide robust solutions.

title: Flipping the Switch to Serverless: How McAfee Modernized ~40% of Its Infra and Halved Compute Costs
abstract: At McAfee, we transformed our data platform by migrating over a third of production workloads to Databricks Serverless and empowering 1,000+ end users through SQL Serverless while unifying governance with Unity Catalog. This shift cut idle cluster overhead, delivered $350K in projected annual savings and drove a 34% increase in active monthly users—all with a lean platform team. Our phased, data-driven migration playbook made adoption safe, reversible and repeatable, using decision dashboards and clear revert-to-classic paths to de-risk modernization. So far, we’ve achieved 39% infrastructure modernization—moving 90% of warehouses and 40% of jobs to SQL Serverless. In this session, we’ll share how we governed workloads with Unity Catalog, selected high-impact migration candidates, overcame serverless limitations, and built dashboards and tagging strategies that earned executive confidence. Attendees will leave with a proven blueprint to scale serverless adoption confidently across their own organizations.

title: Fox One: Video Content Personalization Powered by GenAI
abstract: *Copresenting with Fox. When Fox introduced Fox One, its short-form video platform, we needed to deliver engaging recommendations at launch despite sparse, noisy engagement signals. Rather than waiting months for reliable data, we used GenAI to bootstrap personalization and guide early decision making under real-world constraints.In this talk, we describe how we designed a modular, production-ready personalization system on Databricks that blends GenAI with classical machine learning and evolves as data matures. We will cover how we used Gemini to extract structure from video content, leveraged Databricks Functions to infer early preference signals, built millisecond-level video retrieval on Databricks Lakebase, and developed offline evaluation using GenAI to provide directional insight when traditional A/B testing was unreliable. Attendees will learn how we weaved GenAI into classical recommendation systems on the Databricks lakehouse to ship personalization under data constraints.

title: From "Hidden Costs" to "High Efficiency": Scaling DV's Lakehouse Observability
abstract: A practical guide to the internal monitoring tools we built on Databricks System Tables to make our data platform more efficient and performant. Hear directly from our Data Platform Engineering team about what worked for us and what we learned along the way We’ll dive into the backend strategies that transformed our platform, including: Dashboards We Actually Look At: Tracking real costs and table/column sizes. Practical Alerts for Real Problems: Identifying costly unused tables, tables with missing or excessive retention policies, long-running queries, and constantly failing jobs. The "Orphan" Hunt: Programmatically identifying unreferenced files and un-vacuumed data by auditing Delta logs against cloud storage. The Cost/Read Metric: Using recursive lineage queries to expose expensive, high-maintenance tables with little or no downstream value. Footer Analysis: Scanning Parquet footers at scale to isolate metadata bloat from actual data —even in complex nested types.
description_raw:
A practical guide to the internal monitoring tools we built on Databricks System Tables to make our data platform more efficient and performant. Hear directly from our Data Platform Engineering team about what worked for us and what we learned along the way

We’ll dive into the backend strategies that transformed our platform, including:

  • Dashboards We Actually Look At: Tracking real costs and table/column sizes.
  • Practical Alerts for Real Problems: Identifying costly unused tables, tables with missing or excessive retention policies, long-running queries, and constantly failing jobs.
  • The "Orphan" Hunt: Programmatically identifying unreferenced files and un-vacuumed data by auditing Delta logs against cloud storage.
  • The Cost/Read Metric: Using recursive lineage queries to expose expensive, high-maintenance tables with little or no downstream value.
  • Footer Analysis: Scanning Parquet footers at scale to isolate metadata bloat from actual data —even in complex nested types.

title: From Dashboards to Data Products: MBC Shahid's App-Driven Revolution
abstract: MBC Shahid, the world’s leading Arabic streaming service serving tens of millions of users, has evolved beyond static dashboards to a curated Data Product culture. In this session, discover how we leveraged Databricks Apps, Model Serving and Unity Catalog to operationalize AI for non-technical teams. We will showcase two game-changing internal apps: “Enigma,” which empowers media buyers and leadership teams with predictive show performance, an actionable, interactive real-time voice of the customer and sentiment analysis experience, and a GenAI social media tool that automates viral reel creation by identifying high-impact frames and flagging sensitive content. Join us to learn how adopting a serverless, app-driven architecture is saving thousands of hours, merging the worlds of traditional tabular data, clickstream events, qualitative customer feedback and video data, all on Databricks Lakehouse, while ensuring governance, and delivering massive business value in record time.

title: From Eight Hours to Eight Minutes: Automating A/B Test Analysis on Databricks
abstract: Mobile game studios run multiple experiments simultaneously, but daily insights are often delayed by manual data preparation, inconsistent methods and ad hoc notebooks. This presentation shows how SEGA HARDlight migrated from Tableau to Databricks and built an automated framework for experiment analysis. It ingests definitions and telemetry, applies uniform statistical models and publishes a daily dashboard with an LLM-generated summary and KPI diagnostics. Unity Catalog manages assets and lineage, while Lakeflow streamlines pipelines. MLflow ensures consistent experiment tracking, and AI/BI dashboards with Genie enable natural language exploration. The dashboard locks in results and decisions, creating an auditable record of truth. Early estimates show over eight hours saved weekly for the data team and roughly half a day saved per new query through automation, aiming to double testing capacity — while also boosting trust and consistency across experiments.

title: From Fragmented Data To AI-Driven Portfolio Impact With Digital Operations
abstract: Years of inorganic growth left TK Elevator (TKE) with data silos, inconsistent KPIs and limited ability to steer its services. By consolidating IoT, operational and financial data on a governed Azure Databricks Intelligence Platform — aligning enterprise semantics through the CDP Nexus program — TKE created a single, trusted data foundation from board to field ops. Medallion architecture ensures reliability and reuse, while Unity Catalog provides governance. On this foundation, TKE extended the same semantics and controls to unstructured data such as contracts and service logs, using LLMs to extract risks, obligations and renewal signals. Intelligent agents combine contract insights with operational performance data to support portfolio decisions with human‑in‑the‑loop validation. This session shows how Digital Ops, standardized KPIs and AI‑ready data products leveraging Genie enabled productivity gains and faster, more consistent decisions, setting context for the Session B deep dive.

title: From Fresh Data to Big Impact: Afresh and Lakebase Fuel Retail Innovation
abstract: In grocery retail, every hour on the shelf raises spoilage and margin risk. Afresh combats this with data-driven inventory insights delivered directly to fresh department order writers. But running these workloads on standard PostgreSQL caused contention, table locks, ETL failures and heavy maintenance, limiting innovation.This session details how Afresh migrated its mission-critical Postgres instance to Databricks Lakebase—a fully managed, serverless PostgreSQL engine natively integrated with the Databricks Lakehouse. Using Lakebase’s separation of storage and compute, Afresh eliminated locking and scaling bottlenecks. By orchestrating DBSQL http_request pipelines in dbt, Afresh replaced custom sync frameworks with automated, bidirectional data flows, cutting overhead and boosting reliability.Attendees will learn: How to migrate from self‑managed OLTP to serverless Lakebase Performance and reliability strategies Ways streamlined architecture drives efficiency and innovation

title: From Hours to Seconds: Panasonic’s Global Scale Engineering With the Data Intelligence Platform
abstract: Panasonic’s global footprint—from EV batteries to smart mobility—demands real-time precision. To fulfill the vision of "making tomorrow better," Panasonic transformed its data backbone into a unified Lakehouse. This session explores the engineering feat of unlocking SAP, Salesforce and SharePoint data, slashing SAP data latency from 2 hours to just 5 seconds. Learn how Panasonic is scaling from a single use case to an enterprise-wide strategy, deploying Lakeflow Connect to unify ingestion across multiple data sources and AI Query to structure unstructured data. We’ll also review strategic deployment of Auto Loader and Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines for high-scale CDC and Unity Catalog for cross-domain lineage and governance. And hear how automation and Lakeflow Jobs have eliminated overhead and empowered 10+ global teams. Finally, we’ll preview our roadmap for AI Agents, showing how a robust engineering foundation enables the next generation of autonomous intelligence.

title: From Job Clusters to DBSQL Serverless: How GetYourGuide Improved DBT Pipelines
abstract: At GetYourGuide, we used to run ETL transformations with an internal tool called Rivulus on top of Databricks job clusters. When we standardized on dbt-databricks, we migrated these workloads to DBSQL Serverless and had to rethink how we design and operate our pipelines. We'll walk you through what changed and what we wished to know before we started.What changed for us: Pipelines run ~60% faster and costs dropped ~20% Time to production for new models went from days to minutes, backfills from weeks to hours ~4 months of annual overhead from DBR version upgrades disappeared 100% of our core models are now automatically documented with AI Finance and marketplace teams now rely on fresher data with less friction Attendees will leave with: A clear view of how the migration to DBSQL Serverless was performed How we turned curve balls into wins along the way Concrete steps to apply similar ideas in their own dbt-databricks setups, including using ai_query for automated model documentation
description_raw:
At GetYourGuide, we used to run ETL transformations with an internal tool called Rivulus on top of Databricks job clusters. When we standardized on dbt-databricks, we migrated these workloads to DBSQL Serverless and had to rethink how we design and operate our pipelines. We'll walk you through what changed and what we wished to know before we started.What changed for us:

  • Pipelines run ~60% faster and costs dropped ~20%
  • Time to production for new models went from days to minutes, backfills from weeks to hours
  • ~4 months of annual overhead from DBR version upgrades disappeared
  • 100% of our core models are now automatically documented with AI
  • Finance and marketplace teams now rely on fresher data with less friction

Attendees will leave with:

  • A clear view of how the migration to DBSQL Serverless was performed
  • How we turned curve balls into wins along the way
  • Concrete steps to apply similar ideas in their own dbt-databricks setups, including using ai_query for automated model documentation
areas_of_interest: Developer Experience, Performance & Cost Optimizations

title: From Legacy to Lakehouse: Proven Best Practices From Databricks and McKinsey on Modernization
abstract: As enterprises race to operationalize AI at scale, legacy data warehouses have become a critical bottleneck. In this session, Databricks and McKinsey share proven, real-world best practices for modernizing data platforms to unlock analytics, GenAI and agentic workloads with speed, governance and cost efficiency. Drawing from large scale transformations including McKinsey’s Nebula platform, we outline a pragmatic modernization blueprint that balances risk, payback period and business impact. Attendees will learn how to migrate from brittle legacy architectures to an open lakehouse and federated data mesh, reduce total cost of ownership by up to 40%, accelerate time to insight and enable secure self-service analytics and AI across the enterprise. This talk delivers concrete lessons for executives and practitioners navigating modernization in the AI era.

title: From Oracle Cloud to a Governed Lakehouse: Real-Time Streaming With Spark Declarative Pipelines
abstract: Flutter SEA invested heavily in OCI, but platform limits—high costs, low observability and operational complexity—became blockers for real‑time engagement, event processing and fast delivery. The previous Spark Structured Streaming architecture used custom code with minimal monitoring, no standardized data quality and limited governance, causing slow iteration and high maintenance.This session shares the migration to a governed, observable Databricks Lakehouse using Spark Declarative Pipelines and Delta Lake, built by a “triforce” team: Flutter SEA, Databricks PS and Reply. We’ll cover architectural choices like single‑plex vs. multi‑plex pipelines and their impact on scalability, cost and simplicity, plus data quality, end‑to‑end monitoring, KPIs - 40% savings and 96% bronze-to-silver performance improvements, lessons learned and best practices for modernizing streaming systems to reduce cost, improve governance and enable AI‑driven real‑time engagement.

title: From Power BI DAX to Metric View KPIs: How Mercedes-Benz Korea Built “Talk to Your Data” at Scale
abstract: Mercedes‑Benz Korea runs its business on a rich set of sales and steering KPIs maintained in Power BI semantic models and a central Master KPI catalog. Over time, this BI‑centric implementation made it hard to reuse KPI logic for new AI experiences and across platforms. To enable “Talk to Your Data” on top of governed KPIs, Mercedes‑Benz Korea partnered with Databricks to move KPI logic into the Databricks Lakehouse and Unity Catalog. In this session, we show how we developed an automated DAX‑to‑SQL transpiler that converts existing DAX measures into Unity Catalog Metric Views, while preserving semantic parity with legacy reports. We’ll walk through the architecture, how we use the Master KPI List as the semantic contract, and how we tuned Databricks Genie with KPI‑aware metadata and instructions. Finally, we’ll share lessons from making AI agents production‑ready—from handling complex DAX patterns to rolling out self‑service “talk to your KPIs” capabilities across markets.

title: From Raw Signals to Real-Time Insights: Building an End-to-End IoT Pipeline With Zerobus
abstract: In the world of IoT, every additional hop in your data pipeline is a tax on speed, a risk to governance and a driver of unnecessary cost. To solve this problem, we developed DataLink, an internal use wireless data collection platform that streams high fidelity data directly into Unity Catalog at near real-time speeds. With Zerobus, we built an IoT ingestion pipeline that delivers fast access and robust governance at a fraction of the traditional cost and complexity. This architecture transforms raw lab data from across the globe into structured insights in near real time. In this presentation, we will take a deep dive into our architectural strategy for connecting IoT devices to Zerobus endpoints and setting up ingestion. We will also discuss how we leveraged alerts, dashboards, and automated failure analysis to streamline workflows for engineering teams. Finally, we will walk through a live demo showcasing the end-to-end pipeline from data generation to consumption.

title: From Unstructured Data to Structured Insights: How YipitData Scales Data Enrichment with AI Agents
abstract: YipitData analyzes billions of unstructured data points at petabyte scale to deliver high fidelity insights to institutional investors and Fortune 500 companies. With 100s of heterogeneous data sources, regex, classic ML and NLP techniques never met our accuracy hurdle, limiting our product breadth for years.In this session, we reframe entity resolution as an agentic problem and share our production-grade enrichment platform built on Apache Spark™, Agent Bricks, Vector Search and Lakebase. This AI-native architecture continuously discovers and tags data at 90%–95% accuracy, reliably covering 60,000+ companies—a 20x improvement.Data/ML leaders, engineers and practitioners will learn: Modular, pipeline design applicable to any classification scenario Batch inference patterns in Spark that streamlines infrastructure Techniques for continuous, low maintenance entity discovery Join us and leave with a blueprint to turn enrichment bottlenecks into self-improving, AI pipelines.

title: From Wellhead to Insight: Halliburton’s Real‑Time Data Sharing with Chevron
abstract: How can raw well data be made available more quickly and more consistently across organizations, without relying on manual data movement? In this joint session, Halliburton and Chevron showcase a breakthrough in energy data collaboration. Halliburton shares how they can publish curated well completion and treatment datasets directly into Chevron’s environment using Delta Sharing, enabling more timely visibility into well performance, deferment drivers and optimization opportunities. We’ll walk through the technical architecture that allows Halliburton and Chevron to collaborate securely across Azure and AWS clouds without the latency associated with traditional ETL. We will cover the practical lessons learned during implementation — including establishing robust data contracts and security considerations, and discuss the ROI of Delta Sharing. Attendees will leave with a practical, repeatable understanding on how to deliver trusted, live oilfield data to support more efficient analytics and production decisions, with zero‑copy data sharing.

title: Game Day at Global Scale: How PepsiCo Foods Uses AI to Feed the World Cup
abstract: FIFA World Cup comes to the Bay Area this June. 70,000+ fans at Levi’s Stadium will consume millions of servings of Frito-Lay products, and millions more will be watching across North America. PepsiCo Foods doesn’t get a second chance to stock the right SKUs, in the right quantities, at the right locations. Get it wrong and you have bare shelves. This session shows how PepsiCo Foods runs 125 million demand forecast combinations weekly on Databricks, achieving 80%+ DSD accuracy while reducing compute cost 60% after migrating from on-prem solution. We’ll demo how supply planners model what-if scenarios in real time using Databricks Apps and Lakebase, query demand drivers through Genie, and how agentic AI is beginning to automate anomalous demand detection across Sales, Marketing, Finance and Supply Chain. You’ll learn: How PepsiCo runs driver-based forecasting at 125M combinations/week on Databricks How Lakebase and Apps enable real-time what-if scenario planning

abstract: UBS and LSEG will jointly present how Databricks Unity Catalog and its Delta Sharing capabilities enable LSEG to provide UBS RiskLab: the bank’s model development lab platform, with direct, compliant access to LSEG’s extensive financial data products. This gives UBS data scientists governed, auditable self-service access, accelerating raw data exploration, business intelligence and rapid prototyping of Data and AI solutions under a robust control framework. The UBS RiskLab team will demonstrate how analytics are accelerated by securely combining UBS internal data with LSEG Delta Share data through Unity Catalog, with policy-based entitlements and centralized governance. This approach reduces the lengthy, siloed analysis typically associated with onboarding external data feeds. With the Unity Catalog AI Assistant and Genie, data scientists can quickly and confidently derive insights from a unified view of UBS internal and LSEG external data.

title: Grounding Legacy BI: Etihad Airways’ Transition to AI-Powered Self-Service with Genie
abstract: Modern airlines need more than dashboards. They need intelligence at speed. Etihad Airways is transforming its analytics landscape by moving from traditional BI platforms to Databricks AI/BI, redefining how data is accessed, understood and acted upon across the enterprise. This session tells the story behind that shift, beginning with a bold pilot in Catering that demonstrated the power of generative, natural-language insights and sparked immediate executive engagement from day one. We’ll explore Etihad’s journey from complex, siloed reporting to an AI-first, self-service experience powered by Databricks Genie, turning data into an always-on business co-pilot. Attendees will learn how this transformation accelerated speed-to-insight, reduced technical debt, and scaled rapidly across diverse business units. We’ll conclude with a demo of our current Genie implementation and a forward look at Etihad’s evolving roadmap in the Databricks ecosystem, offering practical lessons for anyone.

title: How Databricks Apps Facilitated Model Retraining
abstract: At Bridgestone, our mission is “serving society with superior quality.” As the world’s largest manufacturer of tire products, Bridgestone has a unique opportunity to leverage data and insights to improve manufacturing efficiency. Bridgestone’s Bandag Retread Solutions has over 65 years leading retread innovations. In this talk, we will focus on how our data scientists used Databricks Apps to improve a computer vision model that facilitates decision-making during the tire retread process. We will show how in 3 weeks, a fully-fledged computer vision labeling application could be built using apps, saving our retread SMEs months of manual effort and thousands of dollars in a 3p software. Our talk will also highlight the benefits of choosing Databricks apps to host our solution: The ease of integration with existing Databricks resources Out of box compliance with internal security standards The speed in developing a front-end application, without the need of managing cloud infrastructure
