title: How Hilton Grand Vacations Optimizes Inventory with AI on Databricks
abstract: Timeshare and hospitality inventory decisions are complex, spanning multiple revenue streams and liabilities, constrained by thousands of contractual and operational rules. This talk explores how HGV’s Inventory AI transformed yield and revenue optimization into an AI-powered, simulation-driven workflow that enforces business guardrails, scores alternative moves, and surfaces real-time revenue impact. Powered by Databricks, the solution builds on our modernization program that migrated thousands of data elements and analytics workloads to Databricks, enabling scalable data engineering and ML operations. We’ll showcase how these components integrate into an AI-first decisioning platform - a conversational interface bridging business users and data science, and the evolution from decision support to semi-autonomous agentic AI for “What If...” simulations that streamline inventory processes. Finally, we’ll share organizational success predictors for enterprise AI adoption.

title: How Mastercard Turns Transaction Data into Trusted Merchant Insights with AI
abstract: We are the first team at Mastercard to build an AI-powered, API-based product on Databricks—GAIME. GAIME is a fuzzy-matching algorithm, which matches transaction/merchant descriptors with Mastercard's real-time merchant database. GAIME consists of entity recognition, candidates retrieval via embeddings + vector search and uses AI-as-a-judge to produce a final match decision. The talk is organized around three foundational pillars of an AI-powered product: governance, responsible AI and quality control. We will discuss how these pillars become accelerators rather than constraints at the regulated financial sector. Finally, we will share the journey of moving GAIME from POC to production and demonstrate what role data science plays in scaling high-impact AI products within a regulated environment.

title: How Nubank Migrated to Databricks SQL Reducing Costs by 40% and Doubling Queries
abstract: At Nubank, we successfully migrated to Databricks SQL over 12 months, addressing the needs of our large-scale data environment with 17k users, 40MM monthly queries, and operations globally serving 100MM+ customers. Main goals: improve cost efficiency, enhance scalability, and maintain business continuity during explosive growth. Key aspects: Zero-downtime throughout the 12-month migration period Successfully scaling platform to handle 100% query growth (20MM to 40MM monthly queries) Comprehensive workload migration supporting 17k users globally +40% annual cost savings Leverage of UC for centralized data governance in highly regulated FSI industry This migration significantly improved our cost structure, enhanced our ability to support business growth and provided a scalable foundation for serving LATAM’s largest fintech, ultimately demonstrating that platform transformation and business velocity can succeed simultaneously in highly regulated environments.

This migration significantly improved our cost structure, enhanced our ability to support business growth and provided a scalable foundation for serving LATAM’s largest fintech, ultimately demonstrating that platform transformation and business velocity can succeed simultaneously in highly regulated environments.

title: How Open Lakehouse Architectures Drive Data and AI Platform Modernization
abstract: At GEODIS, we have launched a complete data and AI cloud platform modernization with the three main objectives. First, reduce Platform engineer workload with a range of best of breed managed services—including Databricks. Second, keep all the development and investment done on our previous architecture "compatible" with the new one—no bing bang. And last and most importantly, keep the platform fully open, interroperable and modular.In that context, we have been able to integrate with our legacy Cloudera technology in MS Azure new components—Astronomer, Privacera, Databricks and Starburst, relying on open standards and technologies—full Apache Iceberg™ data lake, catalog interroparability and Apache Spark™ data quality validation.I propose to discuss the challenges we have faced like Iceberg interroperability—Iceberg v2 vs v3, multi-engine integration—Databricks, Starbrust/Trino and Cloudera, and migration strategy—performance and interroperability enforcement.

title: How Pfizer and REDCap Cloud Modernized Clinical Data Sharing
abstract: Pfizer has transformed clinical trial data exchange by moving from manual, batch-oriented ingestion to a real-time, cross-organizational architecture. Previously, fragmented systems delayed data access and complicated collaboration with research partners. This session explains how Pfizer and RCC used Delta Sharing and UC to enable secure, live data access across different cloud providers and platforms. What you will learn Real-time architecture: move from 24-hr batch cycles to 5-min readiness Cross-cloud sharing: share live data between Azure, AWS and external CDMS platforms without egress complexity Simplified governance: managing strict Life Sciences compliance and data privacy at scale using Unity Catalog Operational efficiency: reducing costs by consolidating legacy orchestration into serverless compute and unified workflows. Interoperability: facilitated direct sharing between REDCap Cloud’s CDMS and Pfizer’s internal analytics environment for faster clinical decision-making

title: How Supercell Uses Databricks to Serve Nearly 300M Monthly Players and Prevent Player Harm
abstract: Supercell, the creator of hits like Clash of Clans and Brawl Stars, aims to build games that are played for years and remembered forever. To achieve this, they need to blend the art of game design with the science of hard facts. Supercell uses Databricks to democratize data access, simplify data processing and enable everyone from game designers to product managers to make hypothesis-driven decisions that keep nearly 300 million monthly players engaged. In this presentation, Supercell will describe their journey with Databricks, highlighting data ingestion with Auto Loader, part of Lakeflow Connect, Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines and Spark Structured Streaming, orchestration with Lakeflow Jobs, governance with Unity Catalog, and the experiences from going all-in on serverless compute, all at massive scale. Presentation culminates in discussing how Trust & Safety team detects and prevents player harm with advanced machine learning algorithms built on these same data foundations.

title: How Unified Data Can Help Secure the Future of Global Food Production
abstract: Agriculture generates vast streams of information—from lab research to satellite imagery, factory sensors and farmers’ mobile apps. Each raw field, whether in a data table or on the farm, carries its own fragment of truth. The real opportunity lies in unifying these fields into connected, trustworthy insight.This keynote shows how data mesh principles and Genie help teams break silos, democratize access and enable proactive decisions that lift yields and strengthen resilience. By bringing together the wisdom hidden across our digital and physical fields, we build a future where every field can help fill every plate.

title: Human-in-the-Loop Compliance at Global Scale: Databricks Apps + Lakebase Casework + SME Labeling
abstract: In this session, we share a practical pattern we call “PolicyOps” on Databricks: converting long policy documents into cited, versioned, queryable policy units, then operationalizing a human‑in‑the‑loop workflow that captures domain SME judgment as structured labels. We’ll show how to parse and structure large policy PDFs into clause-level records (including page-aware citations), how to extract decision criteria into queryable tables, and how to connect policy interpretation to real operational review.We’ll then demonstrate an SME review queue built with Databricks Apps — designed for high-signal feedback (not just thumbs up/down) — with Lakebase used to manage case workflow state (assignment, status, reviewer actions, approvals). The result is a scalable workflow that keeps humans in control of edge cases while systematically converting implicit SME knowledge into reusable labels and corrections that improve quality over time.

abstract: This hands-on course equips learners with the skills to design, implement, and manage catalog structures and large-scale data organization using Unity Catalog in Databricks. Participants will learn to configure secure and scalable storage integrations, as well as implement access controls and isolation strategies for effective data governance. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. title: Inside Adobe’s Near Real-Time Cloud Spend Monitoring System
abstract: Cloud costs are rising faster than budgets, yet teams often discover expensive operations only after the spend has occurred. We moved from reactive cost reporting to proactive, near real-time monitoring—reducing SQL warehouse costs by 51% and Serverless compute by 14% in less than 30 days.This talk covers how we built a production-grade monitoring system managing millions in annual Databricks spend across petabyte-scale data, 94,000+ jobs, and millions of compute hours, with 15-minute monitoring cycles and zero infrastructure cost.The key challenge was attribution: Databricks assigns SQL warehouse costs to warehouse creators, not query executors, obscuring who drives spend. We solved this by combining Databricks system tables—`billing.usage`, `query.history`, and `compute.warehouse_events`—to enable query-level cost attribution without added compute.Attendees will learn the architecture, implementation patterns and reusable strategies to build proactive cost monitoring at any scale.

title: Inside our Lakeflow Journey at SEGA: Scaling Streaming Analytics Across Titles
abstract: At SEGA Europe, we process billions of game telemetry events daily to power real-time analytics for live ops, player experience optimisation, and business intelligence. We have rebuilt our streaming platform on Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines, moving from fragile, manually managed jobs to a resilient, self-healing lakehouse architecture. This approach was battle-tested during the launch of Football Manager 2026, where scale and reliability were critical.This session shares our production journey with Lakeflow—the wins, lessons, and unexpected challenges of operating at gaming scale. We’ll cover our migration from legacy streaming infrastructure, achieving sub-minute latency, and handling evolving event schemas. Topics include stream fan-out from Kinesis to Bronze, schema evolution, late and out-of-order events, data quality checks, performance tuning, and cost control. We’ll also share quantified results from FM26 and practical patterns for teams evaluating Lakeflow at scale.

abstract: Pharma leaders face pressure to accelerate commercialization and improve launch performance, yet many commercial analytics teams are held back by fragmented data, brittle dashboards and heavy manual insight generation. title: Integrating Lakebase Databases into Intelligent Applications
abstract: This hands-on course teaches Databricks application developers how to use Lakebase as the operational database for intelligent, data-driven applications on Databricks. Learners explore how Lakebase enables applications powered by analytics, BI, and AI. Through guided exercises, the course covers Lakebase core concepts and architecture, provisioning and managing database instances, connecting from Databricks SQL, notebooks, and external clients, and applying best practices for connection management, permissions, and environment configuration. By the end of the course, developers will be able to confidently integrate Lakebase into production-ready data and AI applications, safely manage database resources, and unlock unified analytics and AI on live operational data. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces and notebooks Intermediate SQL proficiency, including SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements Intermediate Python programming experience, including functions, exceptions, and working with dictionaries and lists Familiarity with OLTP fundamentals, including client-server relationships, ACID properties, database authentication, and concurrent access

title: Lakeflow Connect: Cutting Enterprise Ingestion Costs by up to 90% on Databricks
abstract: Enterprise CRM-to-lakehouse pipelines built on traditional third-party ingestion tools can be expensive, time-consuming to implement, and often deliver stale data batches. We replaced this with Databricks Lakeflow Connect for a Fortune 500 client — significantly reducing monthly costs, cutting setup time from weeks to hours, and achieving near-real-time CDC ingestion. This talk delivers a live 7-step walkthrough: connector selection, OAuth setup, DLT pipeline config, object selection, Unity Catalog destination mapping, CDC monitoring, and production scheduling. Takeaways include a cost justification framework demonstrating significant multi-year TCO savings, a migration blueprint from legacy and cloud-native ingestion tools, and governance best practices with Unity Catalog lineage.

title: Let Your Agent Pilot Your Fleet: Business Insights From 35,000 Feet
abstract: We run a data platform at Thales IFE that tracks how millions of passengers interact with in-flight entertainment at 35,000 feet. While the data was widely available, business teams still depended on analysts to answer questions or build yet another dashboard. This session shows how we made an AI assistant the primary interface for business and operational insight using Databricks Apps and how it was integrated with existing business systems and portals. We walk through a real scenario: A business user opens a Databricks App, interacts with the AI agent and explores passenger usage & system performance data. The same agent can also be embedded into existing business applications, bringing consistent data access directly into daily workflows. The talk focuses on outcomes and lessons learned: What Databricks simplified, where challenges appeared and what it takes to move AI agents from experiment to production for our business users.

title: Making AI Work: Strategy, Teams and the Human Edge
abstract: When AI goes wrong, you see what really matters. In this highly interactive session, you’ll watch an AI trainwreck unfold as an agent runs amuck without the right guardrails, architecture or roles in place. Then, in a collaborative post‑mortem, we’ll map the missing middle layer of people, process and platform together and design practical fixes. You’ll then act as domain experts to help train an AI Judge that can scale oversight. Expect fewer slides, more doing—and leave with concrete patterns you can apply immediately to make your AI projects safer, faster and genuinely useful.

Expect fewer slides, more doing—and leave with concrete patterns you can apply immediately to make your AI projects safer, faster and genuinely useful.

title: Modern Data Modeling at Scale: Advanced Patterns
abstract: Unlock the truth behind data modeling in Databricks. This session will tackle the top 10 myths surrounding relational and dimensional data modeling. Attendees will gain a clear understanding of what Databricks Lakehouse truly supports today, including how to leverage primary and foreign keys, identity columns for surrogate keys, column-level data quality constraints and much more. Whether you’re migrating from a legacy warehouse or building new analytics solutions, you’ll leave equipped to fully leverage Databricks’ capabilities, and design scalable, high-performance data models for enterprise analytics.

title: Modern Data Warehousing
abstract: This hands-on course is designed for data professionals who want to explore the data warehousing capabilities of Databricks. Assuming no prior knowledge of Databricks, it provides an introduction to leveraging Databricks as a modern cloud-based data warehousing solution. Learners will explore how use the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to ingest, transform, govern, and analyze data efficiently. Learners will also explore Genie, an innovative Databricks feature that simplifies data exploration through natural language queries. By the end of this course, participants will be equipped with the foundational skills to implement and optimize a data warehouse using Databricks. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Basic SQL proficiency and understanding of data querying concepts General knowledge of data warehousing concepts, including tables, schemas, and ETL/ELT processes Some experience with BI or data visualization tools (recommended but not required)

title: Modernization Without Illusions: Migrating a Business-Critical Equities Data Warehouse to Databricks
abstract: Transitioning a mission-critical data platform is rarely straightforward—especially when the legacy system already works and the environment is tightly regulated.This session shares real-world lessons from moving a high-stakes post-trade data platform to a cloud-native Databricks architecture, focusing on the trade-offs that determine whether teams merely migrate or truly land well. We explore how decisions around design, execution, and platform-native patterns shape performance, cost and business SLAs from day one—and why treating migration and modernization as a binary choice often leads to poor outcomes.Through concrete examples, we highlight how principle-driven interventions turned initially underperforming designs into stable, scalable solutions, offering a candid look at the realities of transitioning critical data platforms without sacrificing predictability or value.

title: Modernizing Clinical Data Integration at Novo Nordisk With Lakebase and the Databricks Lakehouse
abstract: Novo Nordisk transformed how vendor-sourced and vendor-facing clinical trial data is ingested, operationalized and exposed by building an API layer on the Databricks lakehouse powered by Lakebase. By replacing fragmented, self-hosted Postgres silos with Lakebase, a Postgres engine integrated with the lakehouse, we now make data available 30x faster than with legacy self-hosted Postgres.This session walks through the end-to-end architecture and migration. We’ll talk about why Lakebase was chosen—horizontal scale and simplified operations—and how these capabilities reduce friction and speed onboarding. We’ll further expand on the blueprints we employed for Lakebase development, including governance and deployment patterns that can be adopted across an organization, and elaborate on the lessons learned along the way. Attendees will learn how we modernized ingestion of structured and unstructured data to achieve consistent, auditable data in a regulated environment.

title: Molecules, Merchants & Models: How Structure-First AI Makes Promotion Intelligence Trustworthy
abstract: The presentation, "Molecules, Merchants & Models: How Structure-First AI Makes Promotion Intelligence Trustworthy," describes a promotion intelligence system built on the Databricks Lakehouse. Retail promotions interact through substitution, complementarity and spillover effects that merchants intuitively understand, but most tools fail to explain.Our system models item and promotion relationships as reusable graph structures—Promo Molecules—learned from over 70 billion rows of transaction data. These structures explicitly surface demand shifts, including substitutes, halo effects and cannibalization. We apply Explainable AI (XAI) at the relationship level—exposing deterministic, merchant-validated signals.Foundation models then reason probabilistically to answer natural-language “what-if” questions. This combined approach enables explainable, merchant-trusted promotion decisions at scale, without manual overrides or black-box predictions.

title: Move Fast, Stay Secure: Enterprise AI Agent Security in Practice
abstract: Agentic AI is moving from POCs to mission-critical workloads- and the attack surface is expanding fast. As LLM-driven agents reason, plan, and act across enterprise data and tools, a new class of risk emerges: the "Lethal Trifecta," where sensitive data access, untrusted inputs, and external actions converge to make prompt injection attacks exploitable end-to-end. Karol Piekarski (Experian) and Nishith Sinha (Databricks) present the Databricks Agentic AI Security Framework (DASF v3), drawing on both Databricks' own AI infrastructure which handles millions of daily queries, and patterns from Fortune 100 deployments. The session walks through how the Databricks platform breaks the Lethal Trifecta at each layer: constraining what agents can access, hardening how they process inputs, and limiting what a compromised agent can actually do. Finally, see how the Databricks Security team built, open-sourced, and actively uses BlackIce, a containerized AI red teaming toolkit, to shift security from a point-in-time exercise to continuous, automated validation. Leave with a practical framework for moving fast on AI without compromising governance or security.

title: Next-Generation Data Platform Transformation
abstract: The Move to Cloud initiative represents far more than a technological migration; it marks a milestone in the bank’s digital transformation journey. Delivered through collaboration across data platform, data engineering, infrastructure, architecture and centralized ingestion, it established a new foundation for data. The program migrated 16 petabytes from Cloudera and ScyllaDB environments to a modern, secure and scalable cloud architecture, increasing speed, efficiency and resilience for business growth and data-driven decisions. It also generated USD 40 million in projected cost avoidance over five years and reduced 216,000 vulnerabilities, representing 38% of the technology landscape, engaging more than 100 organizational areas. More than a project, it transformed how the bank manages and values data, positioning technology as a driver of competitiveness and future innovation.

abstract: As Databricks scales enterprise-wide, IT Operations teams face mounting challenges: controlling analytics costs, maintaining reliability and supporting business-critical workloads. A global semiconductor manufacturer leveraged Databricks SQL and AI functions to transform operations at scale—turning cost control and incident response into repeatable, automated workflows.Databricks SQL and AI functions create an autonomous optimization engine, Unity Catalog ensures enterprise governance and production-safe validation guarantees zero SLA risk.Databricks SQL provides the analytical foundation, enabling deep workload analysis through query metadata, execution plans and table statistics. AI functions layer intelligent reasoning on top, automatically generating optimized SQL, reconstructing data lineage and validating code transformations—all within the platform. These are leveraged to address five key problems and the operations team could save a significant time and achieve cost savings.

title: Operationalizing Fine-Grained Data Security With ABAC at Enterprise Scale
abstract: As Amgen’s analytics platform scaled across teams and data domains, enforcing fine-grained data access through direct user and group entitlements became difficult to operate, govern and audit. Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) enabled a shift to policy-driven access based on business attributes, delivering consistent enforcement, improved scalability and reduced operational overhead across the platform.This session details how ABAC emerged as the next step in Amgen’s data security architecture. We will cover early challenges with third-party access control tools, our migration to Databricks Unity Catalog, and the progressive adoption of dynamic views, row-level filtering and column-level masking. These capabilities laid the groundwork for scalable, attribute-driven access control.Attendees will learn the key architectural choices, implementation of tradeoffs and governance results behind deploying ABAC on Databricks at Amgen.

title: Pipeline to Production in Minutes: How a Leading P&C Insurer Achieved End-to-End DevOps on Databricks
abstract: P&C insurance data platforms must deliver analytics rapidly while meeting stringent governance and regulatory requirements. Although Databricks is widely adopted as the analytics backbone, many P&C organizations continue to struggle with inconsistent deployments, manual promotions and fragmented DevOps practices. This lightning talk demonstrates how a P&C organization implemented true end-to-end DevOps on Databricks using Asset Bundles, Databricks Apps and GitHub Actions. Learn how a Git-driven, declarative deployment model uses Asset Bundles to package notebooks, workflows and DLT pipelines with environment-aware configuration, enabling consistent promotion across multiple environments, while GitHub Actions orchestrates CI/CD pipelines for validation, automated deployment and auditability. The session highlights how Databricks Apps are integrated into the same pipeline to operationalize analytics use cases as secure, production-ready data product governed by Unity Catalog.

title: PRADA Serves Looks and High Quality Business Metrics with Lakebase
abstract: PRADA’s advanced analytics and KPI calculations had evolved across multiple disconnected systems, each serving different teams and use cases. This fragmentation made it difficult to maintain consistent definitions, reuse existing logic, and deliver a single trusted view of performance across the business. As data volumes and reporting demands increased, the organization needed a way to reliably manage and compute thousands of KPIs across regions, product categories, and time periods while still providing fast, decision-ready insights. To address this, Prada implemented a unified analytics architecture supported by Lakebase as its central data foundation. Lakebase consolidated previously siloed data into a single governed and scalable environment, enabling high-volume storage, efficient querying, and low-latency KPI computation. On top of this foundation, a standardized KPI model and microservices-based APIs provided consistent, easily accessible analytics across departments and systems, transforming fragmented reporting into a trusted, enterprise-wide capability for data-driven decision making.

title: Real-Time ML Features for Payment Fraud: Architecture Patterns at Scale
abstract: Checkout.com is a leading global digital payments provider, processing over $300 billion in e-commerce volume annually. Our fraud models depend on fresh features, but batch pipelines limited how quickly we could detect new fraud patterns. In this session, we’ll show how we built real-time feature pipelines using Spark Structured Streaming Real-Time Mode (RTM) on Databricks, reducing feature computation to under a second and unlocking an eight-figure revenue opportunity.We’ll cover our technical approach, including custom stateful aggregations with transformWithStateInPandas, event-time watermarking for late payments, and TTL-based state management for deduplication. We’ll also discuss cost optimization at billion-event scale, and consistency between online and offline features.By incorporating real-time features into our fraud models, we achieved a 2% increase in PR-AUC, translating into millions in prevented fraud losses - offering a practical blueprint for real-time machine learning at scale.

title: Revolutionizing Construction Intelligence: Scaling Procore Analytics With Delta Sharing & AI
abstract: Procore serves as the backbone for global construction data. Historically, providing seamless access to large-scale analytics meant complex ETL pipelines and high egress costs. This session explores how Procore Analytics pivoted to a “zero-copy” architecture using Databricks Delta Sharing. We will detail our Cloud Connector solution, showcasing how we securely share petabyte-scale data via Unity Catalog. We’ll highlight a global enterprise that leveraged this architecture to break down internal silos. By combining Procore capital project data with diverse enterprise datasets, they unlocked high-value use cases with data primed for AI and agent execution. Attendees will learn how the open Delta Sharing protocol enables frictionless “Bring Your Own Warehouse” capabilities, reducing TCO and latency. We’ll share technical lessons on optimizing cross-cloud performance and building an AI-ready foundation for the world’s most complex industry.

title: Rewind, Replay, Recover: Building Operational Resilience at NAB With Spark Declarative Pipelines
abstract: Discover how National Australia Bank (NAB) scales production streaming with Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP), using Streaming Tables and Materialized Views to enable rewind and replay — restoring pipelines to a consistent state, applying fixes safely and reprocessing only affected data. Explore how this approach reduces brittle manual interventions and avoids full refreshes by leaning on declarative semantics, governed data products and robust checkpoint/versioning patterns in the lakehouse. Learn practical patterns to address bad input data, schema drift and logic errors — plus the observability and guardrails that stabilize streaming workloads as they evolve. Walk away with actionable designs, checklists and success criteria for resilient, cost‑efficient streaming at enterprise scale in regulated environments. Outcomes include faster recovery, lower operational effort and cost, improved reliability and correctness and sustained performance as concurrency grows.

title: Running LLaMA at Scale: Production Inference on Databricks Model Serving
abstract: Serving LLMs at consumer scale stresses everything: throughput, latency, correctness and cost. At Superhuman—formerly Grammarly—we built a production system that serves a fine‑tuned LLaMA‑3B model for grammatical error correction to millions of monthly users on Databricks Model Serving. This talk shares what we validated and how: direct‑ingress patterns for high RPS, load testing toward 100K+ QPS targets, multi‑region failover and cold‑start mitigation, and observability. We’ll cover validation checks—A/B tests, golden sets—plus the cost model we used to compare against our internal vLLM-based stack, including levers like dynamic batching, autoscaling and speculative decoding. Expect concrete configs, dashboards and a candid readout of wins, incidents and unit‑economics tradeoffs that informed our ramp from pilot to majority traffic.

title: Safe Skies: Automating Trust at Scale With AI-Generated Contracts at Boeing
abstract: In aviation, "bad data" is an operational risk. For large enterprises with hundreds of data products, manual stewardship stifles innovation. To ensure "safe skies," we transformed governance and quality into engineering disciplines.This session explores our "Governance as Code" architecture using DQX and Ontos on Unity Catalog. We demonstrate using GenAI to bootstrap trust, scanning raw data to auto-generate Open Data Contract Standards (ODCSq). We then show how DQX acts as the enforcer, compiling contracts into pipelines that quarantine violations and alert on drift.AI-assisted contracts: Using LLMs to profile data and draft rigorous ODCS contracts, cutting onboarding time.Automated quality: Deploying DQX to enforce row-level quality, quarantine bad data and trigger drift alerts.Semantics-first: Using Ontos to map technical assets to business meaning for a discoverable data marketplace.Join us to learn how we replaced bureaucracy with automation and engineering rigor.

title: Scaling Agentic AI and Enterprise Data Mesh With Databricks Lakebase
abstract: Zillow is evolving from a search platform into an AI-native "Super App." To move agentic AI from prototype to production, we solved a critical challenge: providing agents with a reliable, high-performance sense of state.Persistent memory is central to this shift. By integrating Databricks Lakebase, we’ve bridged the gap between the data lake and the millisecond latency real-time agents require. This allows our AI to maintain long-term, vectorized context—enabling agents to remember, learn and act with precision.As Lakebase adoption grows, it is catalyzing enterprise-wide innovation. By powering real-time segmentation and communications, it reduces architectural friction and accelerates our Data Mesh strategy, paving the way for Zillow’s next generation of real-time data.

title: Scaling AI Agents at Mercedes-Benz: Unified AI Governance in a Multi-Cloud Enterprise Ecosystem
abstract: Managing AI at the scale of Mercedes-Benz requires more than just models—it requires a unified AI ecosystem. With thousands of practitioners across global business units, the challenge was providing a single point of entry for AI capabilities while maintaining strict governance across multi-cloud and multi-model environments. In this session, we go under the hood of the Mercedes-Benz AI Ecosystem. We will detail the technical architecture that integrates Databricks Agent Framework, Model Serving, and AI Gateway to create a governed interoperability for AI agents. We’ll discuss the "how-to" of: Centralizing discovery: Building an internal marketplace for foundation models and reusable agents Unified governance: Integrating Mercedes-Benz AI Marketplace into Databricks AI Gateway Operationalizing agents: Deploying and publishing AI agents for self-service at Mercedes-Benz Attendees will leave with a technical blueprint for building an interoperable enterprise AI agent platform.

title: Scaling Databricks Genie 10x at GSK Global Supply Chain: Building a Production AI/BI Factory
abstract: Most organizations don't struggle to prototype conversational AI—they struggle to get it reliably into production at scale. At GSK's Global Supply Chain, operating across 37 GMP-regulated manufacturing sites with thousands of daily decisions, we built an AI/BI factory designed to move Databricks Genie from early experimentation to enterprise-grade production—serving analysts, planners and decision-makers across a complex, regulated environment.This talk explores the journey from pilot to platform: how we scaled Genie 10x by treating it not as a standalone tool, but as a production system embedded in a broader agent operating model—one that fuses technology, process, governance and organizational ways of working into a repeatable delivery engine.What we’ll cover: Genie Spaces lessons learned and best practices Enterprise observability dashboard for Genie CI/CD migration and deployment automation for DevOps Semantic caching for Genie

title: Scaling Energy Deal Evaluation With Databricks Lakebase
abstract: Quantum Capital Group screens hundreds of energy investment opportunities each year, supported by more than 1.5 billion records across public, premium and proprietary datasets. While its Databricks Lakehouse already provided a scalable and governed foundation, the next challenge was enabling teams and downstream tools to work more efficiently from the same trusted data as deal volume and complexity increased. In this session, Quantum shares how it extended its Lakehouse with Databricks Lakebase to introduce relational structure and familiarity without sacrificing openness or scale. Attendees will learn how Lakebase integrates with Delta Lake, Unity Catalog and Lakeflow to support governed, materialized datasets that teams can access using SQL, geospatial tools and existing analytics platforms. The talk focuses on how relational access and governance helped Quantum scale human-led diligence across engineering and investment teams, evaluate complex deals with greater confidence.

title: Scaling Geospatial Analytics at S&P Global Energy: From Billions of Points to AI-Powered Map Agents with Databricks
abstract: At S&P Global Energy, we transformed our geospatial analytics architecture to handle billions of records with query results in seconds — removing the need for separate geospatial databases and reducing costs using our Oil & Gas well production data. Architecture evolution: Unified single-source-of-truth on Delta Lake without redundant geospatial systems Databricks Spatial SQL with native spatial types enabling performant queries Databricks SQL Serverless delivering on-demand H3 aggregations Interactive Databricks app demo for large-well datasets. What’s next – AI geospatial agent (work in progress): Natural language map interactions powered by Agent Bricks Genie-driven text-to-SQL for dynamic map layer creation 50+ analytical tools like chart generation from spatial queries Attendees will learn practical patterns for scaling geospatial workloads, leveraging Spatial SQL with native spatial types, H3 indexing, and see how AI agents are reshaping spatial analytics workflows.

  • Databricks Spatial SQL with native spatial types enabling performant queries

Interactive Databricks app demo for large-well datasets.

Attendees will learn practical patterns for scaling geospatial workloads, leveraging Spatial SQL with native spatial types, H3 indexing, and see how AI agents are reshaping spatial analytics workflows.

title: Scaling Trustworthy AI: Takeda’s Self-Service AI Foundation on Databricks
abstract: Takeda and ZS share a bold vision for the future of enterprise AI—one where innovation scales through a secure, self-service AI foundation with governance built in by design. In this session, Takeda and ZS share how they are building a unified AI foundation on Databricks, enabling teams to develop, deploy and operate AI confidently in a highly regulated environment. We will walk through Takeda’s AI foundation architecture—built on the Databricks Mosaic AI framework and leveraging managed model serving endpoints and enterprise wide vectorization patterns—while also demonstrating how automated, compliant project provisioning and Takeda-specific responsible AI guardrails enable secure, scalable delivery of AI-driven applications. Attendees will learn how Takeda’s self-service AI foundation accelerates experimentation, improves reuse and reduces operational friction while strengthening trust, audit readiness and enterprise-wide adoption of AI.

title: Securing the Databricks Lakehouse — a Multi-Layered Defense Against Outside and Inside Threats
abstract: As organizations transition mission-critical workloads to the Databricks Lakehouse Platform on AWS, the traditional “castle-and-moat” security model is no longer sufficient. This presentation outlines a comprehensive, defense-in-depth strategy designed to protect data assets from both external adversaries and internal risks. By synthesizing identity federation, network hardening and dynamic governance, organizations can achieve a “Zero Trust” posture that scales with their data growth.

title: Serverless at Bank Scale: NAB's Complete Workload Migration to Serverless at Enterprise Scale
abstract: Explore how National Australia Bank (NAB) adopted Databricks Serverless across notebooks, jobs, declarative pipelines, SQL Warehouses and Model Serving—delivering platform reliability, cost efficiency and performance without compromising security or governance. Hear NAB’s blueprint: phased migration, workload prioritization and cross‑team operationalization. Learn the essentials—Unity Catalog for fine‑grained governance, plus Private Link and Secure Egress Gateway for private connectivity—and hard‑won lessons from uplifting Spark to declarative pipelines (DLT/LDP), moving SQL to serverless warehouses, and operationalizing model serving with observability and guardrails. Walk away with actionable patterns, checklists and success criteria for regulated environments. Outcomes include broad serverless adoption, faster MLOps cycles, better SQL performance, lower ingestion cost and sustained query scale—a pragmatic path to bank‑grade serverless at enterprise scale.

title: SQL Programming and Procedural Logic
abstract: In this hands-on course, you will explore core SQL programming and procedural logic skills within Databricks, focusing on how to build modular, maintainable, and production-ready analytics workflows. You’ll start by learning about key SQL constructs—such as Common Table Expressions, temporary views, and User Defined Functions—that make your SQL development cleaner and more reusable. The course then guides you through advanced SQL scripting techniques using compound statements, variables, control flow, conditionals, loops, and robust error handling to create structured and flexible SQL processes. Finally, you’ll discover how to orchestrate and modernize complex workflows with Lakeflow Jobs and migration strategies, transforming legacy tasks into modular, automated pipelines that leverage the full capabilities of the Databricks platform. Note: Hands-on training courses will be updated to reflect the newest product and feature announcements from Data + AI Summit in June 2026. Prerequisites Familiarity with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, including workspaces, SQL warehouses, and notebooks Experience with Databricks Lakehouse architecture, including Delta Lake and Unity Catalog Intermediate SQL experience, including stored procedures, dynamic queries, and data analysis in relational databases Familiarity with data pipeline orchestration and workflow management Working knowledge of data warehousing concepts, including ETL, schema design, data modeling, and BI reporting

title: Stop Arguing About Numbers: How Zalando Scales Governance With Unity Catalog and Metric Views
abstract: At Zalando’s scale—serving over 50M European customers via a decentralized microservices architecture—data governance quickly turns into a "Data Swamp." Domain teams shipped fast, but without a unified semantic layer, "Net Revenue" on a marketing dashboard rarely matched the finance report. We faced a familiar dilemma: centralize and create bottlenecks, or stay decentralized and lose trust in our numbers.This session will focus on our implementation of Unity Catalog, scaled across an organization with over 1,000 data engineers. This setup fosters team autonomy, enabling rapid data creation. We will also detail the governance mechanisms we've put in place to facilitate data sharing while maintaining minimal compliance risk.We’ll then go deep into how we use metric views to move business logic out of siloed BI tools and directly into the lakehouse. This metric-as-code approach ensures that whether users query via notebooks, dashboards or AI agents, they get the same trusted answer.

title: Streaming at Scale With Real-Time Mode: Sub-Second Train Telemetry Across the Netherlands
abstract: In this talk, we showcase how NS (Dutch Railways) built an end-to-end Databricks solution that processes train telemetry in real time, enabling live detection of carriage failures and improving overall reliability. The platform handles more than 100 billion data points per day with sub-second end-to-end latency.The session features a live demo of real train telemetry, giving the audience a direct, in‑room experience of real time.We explain how the solution was engineered using the new Real-Time Mode for Spark Structured Streaming. The entire stack is built in PySpark and SQL, so no microservices, no separate streaming framework, just Databricks.Throughout the session, we connect to other high velocity domains with cases like real time parcel rerouting, live airport baggage tracking and real-time signals feeding analytics and AI. Attendees leave with practical insights into bringing real time data into the modern data stack without the complexity of traditional streaming systems.

title: Streaming Data for Operational Apps: Building the Bridge From Analytics to Serving
abstract: Real‑time operational applications need instant query responses, but analytical platforms optimize for throughput over latency. Running separate operational and analytical databases leads to duplicate infrastructure, complex sync logic and data consistency issues. We needed sub‑second dashboards for factory operators to track machine performance in real time, while also enabling historical analysis and ML on the same data. Our previous platform stored only four days of raw data and lacked proper engineering practices. We built a streaming architecture on Databricks that serves operational queries via Lakebase Postgres and analytical workloads via Delta Lake from a single pipeline, eliminating the dual‑database problem. This session covers the architecture, our custom streaming sink, validation of the new platform alongside the legacy system, early mistakes and patterns for complex state logic, exactly‑once delivery, and measurable production outcomes.

title: Streaming the Agency: Databricks-Powered Contest Management, Scalable Dashboards and GenAI Chatbots
abstract: Insurance agents increasingly require fast, accurate and scalable insights to drive sales and service performance. However, legacy contest systems, performance-limited dashboards and rule-based chatbots create delays, manual effort and constrained scalability. This presentation showcases how Databricks’ advanced data engineering like live streaming and DLT that come with near real-time ETL enabled a modernized CMS, scalable dashboards and an intelligent GenAI chatbot. By replacing it with streaming pipelines, we improved contest flexibility, accelerated deployment and delivered timely progress updates. A new dashboard framework now supports 25,000 agents with 1,200 concurrent users. The GenAI chatbot automates content understanding, enabling accurate Q&A, product comparison and pre meeting preparation. The result: data delivered to frontline agents within five seconds, 50% faster contest setup and a more empowered, efficient distribution across organisation.

title: Streaming the Game: Scaling Real-Time Intelligence at DraftKings With Kafka, Databricks & Sigma
abstract: DraftKings set out to build a real-time analytics capability before the use cases were fully defined. Rather than waiting for perfect requirements, we invested in the streaming foundation first — allowing meaningful use cases to emerge from the platform itself.We assembled an initial solution to deliver second-level visibility into transactions, active users, and revenue. As stakeholders gained live insight, new needs surfaced — including real-time liability tracking and deeper financial monitoring — many not originally envisioned. As adoption expanded, we evolved the architecture to Kafka, Databricks Spark Declarative Pipelines and Real-Time Mode, as well as Sigma to improve scalability, flexibility, and governance. In this session, we will review the architectural evolution, key streaming design decisions, and how we blend real-time, historical, and forecast data into a financial intelligence system.Attendees will leave with practical lessons for designing streaming-first systems when requirements are evolving.

title: Streaming to Lakehouse: How Launchmetrics Simplified Pipelines, Built Data Trust and Adopted Genie
abstract: At Launchmetrics, a leader in Brand Performance for fashion, luxury and beauty, our AWS real-time streaming architecture delivered low latency but created growing complexity, data inconsistencies and rising infrastructure costs.We shifted to a batch-first Databricks Lakehouse built on Delta Lake, using medallion architecture and Unity Catalog for governance. The impact exceeded expectations: fewer pipelines, predictable costs, safe historical recomputation, and stronger data reliability—restoring trust across product, commercial and leadership teams.Most importantly, this foundation enabled company-wide adoption of AI/BI Genie. Today, non-technical stakeholders query the lakehouse in natural language, reducing dependency on the data team and accelerating decisions.In this session, we’ll share how we evaluated real-time vs. batch trade-offs, executed the migration safely and designed a “Genie-ready” lakehouse that delivers measurable business value.

title: The Data Gap in Women's Football & How FIFPro and Databricks Are Helping to Close It
abstract: As the FIFA World Cup kicks off in the USA days before this summit, billions will follow the men's game, but women footballers still fight for visibility and basic protections. FIFPro, representing 65,000+ professional footballers, is using data to change that.For over a decade, FIFPro has surveyed women footballers worldwide on working conditions, welfare and employment rights. But that data lived on laptops in fragmented spreadsheets with critical insights locked away.In this session, Blend shares how we built a data platform on Databricks to power evidence-based advocacy. We'll cover designing a medallion architecture for messy survey data spanning multiple tournament cycles, configuring AI/BI Genie so non-technical researchers can query data in natural language, and building Power BI dashboards that surface cross-confederation trends.When women's football data sits on laptops, it's easy to overlook. In a lakehouse, it's impossible to ignore.

title: The Lakehouse Behind a Modern CDP
abstract: Building a CDP isn’t just a UI and APIs—it’s a data platform problem at scale. This talk shows how Warner Bros. Discovery built the Databricks-powered “back of house” for Mosaic, supporting audience segmentation and activation across millions of subscribers. We’ll walk through the data plane that converts high-volume first-party events (client SDK + server events) into governed Delta tables using Spark Structured Streaming, then runs incremental batch pipelines to produce custom attributes and curated event views for segmentation across identity grains. We’ll also cover the audience evaluation engine: drag-and-drop audience definitions are stored as rule trees, compiled into Spark plans and evaluated at scale to generate deterministic audience outputs for downstream delivery. Key production patterns include table modeling, join/partition strategies, observability and separation between the metadata plane (API-driven config) and the Databricks execution plane.

title: The Open Handshake: Scaling Multi-Cloud Delivery via Delta Sharing and Iceberg
abstract: Client demand is shifting. They don’t want our data; they want it in their platform. We solved the “Vendor Lock-in” crisis by building an Open Data Passport. This session deconstructs how we leveraged Delta Sharing and Apache Iceberg UniForm to deliver high-scale healthcare products to clients without moving a single byte of raw data. We’ll share the engineering “scars” of managing cross-cloud permissions and how we turned an interoperability headache into a competitive product advantage for our global ecosystem. Demo: A live cross-platform “handshake” between a Databricks source and a non-Databricks consumer with zero ETL. Takeaways: Architecting zero-copy delivery via Delta Sharing Managing multi-platform governance Building vendor-agnostic data products

title: The Write Way: Building Bi-Directional Data Pipelines in the Lakehouse
abstract: Ever wondered how to bridge the gap between analytical data and operational workflows without breaking your lakehouse architecture? What if business users could edit data directly in a web app while maintaining a single source of truth? Traditional lakehouses excel at analytics but struggle with user-driven updates, often creating silos or complex workarounds.There’s a better way—in this session we’ll cover a full writeback loop where lakehouse (Unity Catalog) syncs to PostgreSQL (Lakebase) for operational speed, a Databricks app offers an intuitive editing interface and Lakeflow jobs sync changes back every 5 minutes.The lakehouse stays the source of truth while users get flexibility. We’ll explore scenarios including form-based data entry, table editing and data quality workflows, all built with Dash and Databricks Asset Bundles. By the end, you'll have a clear blueprint for building bi-directional data flows that empower users without sacrificing governance or reliability.

title: Transforming Governance Into a Scalable Data & AI Capability in the Data Mesh Era
abstract: As enterprises scale AI and data products across domains, traditional governance models fail to keep pace. Historical approaches to data ownership no longer flex to meet data product demands. Domain autonomy creates inconsistency. AI amplifies risk. This presentation presents a scalable operating model that integrates data literacy, enterprise governance, governance engineering and domain enablement into a single cohesive approach built on the modern data platform. Using a federated data mesh architecture powered by Databricks, this modern approach to data ownership transforms governance from documentation and oversight into a programmable, platform-embedded enterprise capability.The result: Faster domain onboarding Reduced regulatory risk Automated policy enforcement Measurable AI readiness

title: Translating Nubank’s Data Lake Access Control Model Into Databricks Unity Catalog
abstract: Nubank implemented consistent, fine-grained access control across its multi-platform data environment by translating its internal data lake access control model into Databricks Unity Catalog permissions. This model uses centrally managed "Permission Groups" (based on geography, data domains and column sensitivity) to govern access to over 300K tables for 5K+ users, enforcing control at the table and column level. The presentation will detail the architectural implementation, the programmatic translation process using Unity Catalog APIs, and the application of read-time masking for sensitive columns. It will address practical challenges in modeling fine-grained permissions and operating at scale, providing attendees with a reusable architectural pattern and concrete lessons for designing scalable, enterprise-grade access control on Databricks.

At the Data + AI Summit, i'll share hard-won lessons from building privacy-aware, access-controlled data lake platforms in a fast-moving fintech environment.

title: Unified Governance for Enterprise Analytics and AI
abstract: We implemented a unified governance architecture for enterprise analytics leveraging Databricks Unity Catalog as the authoritative control plane. The platform designed with a configuration-driven, dynamic attribute-based access control (ABAC) model with fully enforced row-level security (RLS) and column-level security (CLS) ensuring precise, policy-driven data access across the organization — including hierarchical visibility for cost owners and business leaders aligned to their reporting structures. This design prevents governance drift, reduces operational complexity, centralizes policy management, and enables secure self-service analytics across domains. Governance policies extend natively to AI/BI dashboards and Databricks Genie, ensuring consistent, compliant data access without duplicating or redefining security frameworks. Impact Eliminated decentralized access control models across 40+ dashboards. Enabled secure self-service analytics for 300+ users across 20+ teams.

title: Unlocking Antibody Space With Databricks Vector Search
abstract: Antibodies form the bedrock of human health, and the body’s ability to create what is estimated to be a quintillion unique variants creates an impressive scaling issue for their study. Molecules derived from antibodies are now the fastest growing therapeutic category, and our understanding of how to design and modify antibodies requires solutions that scale. Databricks Vector Search provides an engineered solution for scaling up similarity search to 1 billion embeddings per endpoint, and by embedding antibody sequences with the protein language model AMPLIFY we will describe how this system might be used with the Observed Antibody Space dataset. The potential throughput of this system, at ~30 queries returned per second, would demonstrate state-of-the-art search. Using this scalable approach, we would then be positioned to explore advantages that this embedded representation affords for metadata lookup and statistical analysis.

title: Using AI to Migrate Semantic Models and Dashboards to Databricks Metric Views and AI/BI
abstract: At HP, we are working to integrate more of our business intelligence and analytics directly into the Databricks platform. To minimize impacts on analytics teams and their partners across organizations, we needed a way to automate and accelerate migrations of legacy semantic models and dashboards into Databricks. We developed a scalable AI based approach to parse existing BI artifacts, translate semantic models into Metric Views, convert existing visualizations into Databricks Dashboards and validate the results. This presentation shares lessons learned across multiple migrations, including both our key challenges and successes.

title: Using Databricks Geospatial Processing at Scale
abstract: Our team started on Geospatial journey with Databricks DBR 16.X, from legacy project migration and continued to expand by ingesting and processing three different geospatial datasets (HD USHR maps, public OSM and curated OSM (Overture)). We applied different optimization techniques to make spatial joins more performant and worked closely with Databricks Geospatial team in testing out incremental improvements in DBR, culminating in large scale performance increase on DBR 17.X bringing support to geospatial data types (GEOMETRY, GEOGRAPHY) as dedicated data types that could be stored in delta table. A significant optimization was observed in spatial joins, which could eliminate previously used optimization techniques, making data pipelines maintenance easier. All of these improvements enabled us to enrich internal datasets based on various road attributes from these datasets and use them in analytics, training and potential reinforcement learning applications where needed.

title: Weaving Data Into the Financial Fabric: How Nasdaq Uses Databricks to Power the Innovation Economy
abstract: Nasdaq is the trusted fabric of the world’s financial system, and data is at the heart of everything we do. Learn how we’re using Databricks to drive insights, support decision-making and deliver industry-leading financial solutions. Using Delta Lake’s open formats and Unity Catalog, we’ve consolidated disparate data stores into a unified corporate data lakehouse to create a single source of truth for enterprise data. We’ll demo how we’re using AgentBricks and Databricks Apps to democratize access to data while maintaining oversight and governance.You’ll also discover how our Capital Access Platforms division has built its core data platform on Databricks. With exchange and index data, alternative datasets and institutional data for more than 110,000 public and private‑market strategies and funds covering over $90 trillion in institutional assets, the platform enables rapid product development, creates value from our data assets and forms the foundation of our AI strategy.

speakers.name: Angie Ruan
speakers.bio: Angie Ruan is the Divisional CTO for Nasdaq Capital Access Platforms. She is responsible for overseeing the division’s technology strategy, including engineering across Listings, Index, Data, Analytics, Investor Relations, and Governance Solutions. She rejoined Nasdaq after serving as SVP at Chime. Prior to Chime, Angie was SVP of Global Technology at Nasdaq, overseeing key Market Technology engineering. Angie has a strong track record of leading business transformations across American Express, PayPal, and eBay. She holds four technical patents, a Master of Science in Computer Science from UC Santa Barbara, and studied undergraduate in Computer Science at Tsinghua University of China.

title: What Worked, What Didn’t: Virgin Atlantic’s Flight to Trusted Generative BI
abstract: As decision-making at Virgin Atlantic sped up, our BI stack creaked. Leaders wanted answers in hours and questions increasingly cut across commercial, operational, and customer data. Like many teams, we turned to GenBI to close that gap. Our first attempt didn’t go well. Answers arrived faster, but confidence dropped. Without shared metric definitions, visible lineage, or governance designed in from the start, ambiguity scaled faster than trust. “Is this right?” became the default question again. In this session, we share how we rebuilt GenBI on trusted foundations using Databricks. We’ll show how Unity Catalog and UC Metrics Views anchor AI answers to signed-off metrics, how Databricks Genie enables users to interrogate and explain results, and why explainability became part of the user experience. Attendees will leave with practical lessons for moving to decision-grade GenBI, drawn from real production use cases and delivered as a visual, story-led case study.

title: When Redis Isn’t the Answer: Serving Lakehouse Data at Scale
abstract: As our feature and operational use cases grew, maintaining separate Redis/DynamoDB stacks alongside the data lake added latency, skew and ops burden. At Superhuman (formerly Grammarly), we adopted Lakebase to serve low‑latency data directly from Databricks, simplifying our architecture while meeting strict SLAs. This session details our setup and results: how we modelled entities and keys, tuned Lakebase capacity and read replicas for high QPS and enforced freshness with Change Data Feed–driven syncs. We’ll show code and configs for provisioning online stores, publishing tables, and querying endpoints, plus before/after latency and maintenance metrics. Attendees will leave with reference patterns to consolidate online serving on Lakebase without sacrificing performance.

title: Why It's Time to Take AI/BI Seriously
abstract: I'm a Power BI developer by trade, and for years I've refused to engage with other data visualization platforms. Every time I did, I just found it was lacking capabilities or had serious deficiencies compared to Power BI. Then 2025 hit and Databricks invested heavily in improving AI/BI, metric views and Genie. Now, AI/BI is ready to be a legitimate challenger in the Data Visualisation space.In this talk I will show you how you can use AI/BI, metric views and Genie to get the most out of your data. We will start from basics, building a metric view, using it to build a dashboard and then setting up a Genie space. I'll then walk you through my top tips on how you can get the most out of each of them. We'll talk about the types of work where AI/BI thrives and discuss where I see it going in its pursuit of being the next big data visualization platform.AI/BI is here and it's ready to be taken seriously.

title: Wish It. Find It. Query It—How Rooms To Go Connects AI Assistants to the Databricks Data Platform
abstract: At DAIS 2025, Rooms To Go showed how we brought Databricks AI/BI Genie to 700+ warehouse and sales users through Microsoft Teams. This year, we go further. Using Model Context Protocol (MCP), we now connect multiple AI assistants—ChatGPT, Azure Copilot, Claude and Azure AI Foundry—to three core Databricks capabilities: Wish It—Genie for natural-language analytics, Find It—vector search for knowledge retrieval and Query It—Databricks SQL for governed data access. This 40-minute session walks through the real architecture, live demos and lessons learned from giving business users the freedom to choose their AI interface while keeping all data governed on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. Attendees will leave with a concrete playbook for implementing MCP-based AI assistant integrations in their own organizations.

title: Your AI Strategy is Only as Good as Your People Strategy
abstract: As AI moves from experimentation to enterprise impact, success depends on workforce readiness, not just technology. Market leaders have upskilled over 50% of their employees and are redesigning workflows around AI, while 60% of organizations still struggle to generate value from their investments. In this session, Robin Sutara and Melanie Botha explore how to build an AI-enabled workforce by aligning culture, people, process and enablement with your data strategy. You'll learn how to assess your data and AI maturity, close critical skills gaps at scale through structured learning programs, and integrate AI agents into teams in ways that elevate human talent. Drawing on customer proof points and the Databricks data and AI maturity framework, this session provides a practical roadmap for treating upskilling as a core investment—so every project becomes a data and AI project and every employee contributes to AI-driven outcomes.

speakers.name: Robin Sutara
speakers.bio: "From repairing Apache helicopters near the Korean DMZ to the corporate battlefield, Robin has demonstrated success in navigating the high stress, and sometimes combative, complexities of data-led transformations. She has consulted with hundreds of organizations on data strategy, data culture, organizational design, and operational efficiency. Robin has had an eclectic career in technical and business functions with more than two decades in tech companies, including Microsoft and Databricks. Throughout her career, Robin has always supported creating an inclusive workplace. She was recognized in 2023 as a Top 20 Women in Data and Tech, DataIQ 100 Most Influential People in Data, and WomenTech speaker of the Year Finalist. Robin has also founded multiple organizations focused on more female representation in leadership roles and is the Chair of Women in Data - US Chapter."

speakers.name: Melanie Botha
speakers.bio: Melanie leverages 25+ years of experience to guide organizations in building data-driven cultures and scale initiatives. A trusted advisor to C-suite executives, she believes technological transformation requires organizational and cultural evolution. Her team empowers customers, partners, and internal stakeholders to maximize their data and AI potential. Previously, at AWS ANZ, she led programs that skilled over 200,000 individuals, including pioneering GenAI training for non-technical audiences. At Microsoft South Africa, she managed Consumer Channels Group and Marketing and Operations. Earlier roles spanned across Europe and Africa. Melanie is a qualified Art Therapist supporting individuals to overcome mental health challenges.
